# RandNLA CACM Theorem Ledger

This is the live theorem ledger for the full-paper formalization pass for
Petros Drineas and Michael W. Mahoney, "RandNLA: Randomized Numerical Linear
Algebra," Communications of the ACM 59(6), 80--90, 2016.
https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/2842602

The companion backlog
[`RANDNLA_CACM_NOT_PROVED_LEDGER.md`](RANDNLA_CACM_NOT_PROVED_LEDGER.md)
is the authoritative list of open paper-level results. The proof-source ledger
[`RANDNLA_CACM_PROOF_SOURCE_LEDGER.md`](RANDNLA_CACM_PROOF_SOURCE_LEDGER.md)
records the external source chain for incomplete paper proofs and the chosen
proof routes before new Lean infrastructure is added. This file is broader: it
records the extracted algorithms, equations, assumptions, random variables,
events, theorem targets, Lean names, hypothesis classifications, and next proof
steps. A claim is closed only when the corresponding Lean theorem exists and
the proof does not assume the target event, concentration inequality, or
perturbation theorem.

## Status Legend

- `Closed`: a matching Lean theorem exists and appears in the lookup docs.
- `Scoped closed`: a mathematically precise subclaim is proved, but the
  broader survey-level claim remains open.
- `Conditional transfer`: a theorem correctly transfers a separately proved
  event or bound; it does not prove that event.
- `Open`: the paper-level claim is not yet proved in Lean.
- `Documentation only`: narrative or application guidance that needs a
  narrowed theorem target before formalization.

## Global Objects and Assumptions

| Object | Source role | Lean representation | Classification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Matrix dimensions | Deterministic parameters in all algorithms | `m n r q s : Nat`, finite index types such as `Fin m -> Fin n -> Real` | Domain assumption |
| Sample count | Deterministic parameter in Algorithms 1 and 2 | `s` or `steps` | Source/domain assumption; positive or nonzero casts appear where division by `s` is used |
| Input matrix | Deterministic input | `A : Fin m -> Fin n -> Real` | Source assumption |
| Failure/error tolerance | Deterministic accuracy parameter | `epsilon`, `delta`, `eta`, `Q`, `lam` | Source/domain assumption; positivity hypotheses are explicit |
| Floating-point model | Inexact-arithmetic parameter | `fp : FPModel`, `gamma fp k`, `gammaValid fp k` | Floating-point validity assumption |
| Squared-magnitude entry law | Algorithm 1 distribution | `sqMagProb A i j` and `sqMagTraceProbability` | Closed exact distribution, requires nonzero Frobenius denominator |
| Row-norm law | Algorithm 2 equation (4) distribution | `rowSqNormProb A i`, `rowSqNormTraceProbability` | Closed exact distribution, requires nonzero Frobenius denominator |
| Leverage-score law | Algorithm 2 equation (6) distribution | `leverageScoreProb U i`, `leverageTraceProbability` | Closed under `HasOrthonormalColumns U` and `0 < n` |
| Algorithm 3 preprocessing matrices | Random matrices in the paper, deterministic after sampling in Lean | `PiL`, `PiR` in `Preconditioning.lean` | Source object; distribution-specific random claims remain open |
| Algorithm 3 Rademacher signs | SRHT-style random signs used before a Hadamard/orthogonal transform and finite signed-mixing rows | `RademacherTrace`, `rademacherTraceProbability`, `signedMixingRows`, `signedMixingUniformRowSampleProbability`, `signedMixingUniformRowTraceProbability` in `Preconditioning.lean` and `UniformRowSamplingComposition.lean` | Uniform finite sign law, first/second sign moments, signed-linear-form MGF factorization, cross-second-moment identity, scalar Hoeffding tails including the optimized variance-proxy two-sided form, its finite-family union wrapper, the squared-norm vector consequence, exact signed-mixing row-family concentration under deterministic coefficient caps and orthonormal columns, exact signed-mixing one-row mean identity under column-square normalization of `G` and directly under `G^T G = I`, exact signed-mixing orthonormality preservation under `G^T G = I`, exact signed-mixing product-law composition with iid uniform row sample-Gram concentration under a uniform entry-square cap, concrete computed-preconditioner formation for finite signed mixing via rounded `G * diag(sign)` with exact-factor componentwise error order \(\Theta(\gamma_m |G_{ij}|)\), coordinate-Hoeffding row-norm and leverage-probability auxiliaries, the induced uniform one-step Loewner-boundedness event, iid uniform sample-average concentration in tail-budget form, product-law preprocessing-plus-sampling composition, FP uniform-sketch transfer, deterministic FP-budget cap for the scoped route, probability-one signed-preprocessing event, flat-Hadamard expected squared row norm, source-directed expected Euclidean row norm, deterministic row-norm convexity and Lipschitz inputs, deterministic signed-Hadamard row-norm positive-flip self-bounding, deterministic Ledoux-to-Tropp affine scaling constants, finite Bernoulli coordinate expectation/entropy formulas, fair-Bernoulli coordinate log-Sobolev inequality, finite `L2` section-norm reverse-triangle bridge, one-coordinate product peel-off, abstract Bernoulli-product induction lift, finite product-law entropy chain-rule algebra, the finite subgaussian Chernoff MGF-to-tail optimizer, the positive-drop exponential-tilt self-bounding route, source-sharp SRHT row-norm/leverage uniformization, exact uniform-row sampling composition, and source-sharp FP constant-budget transfer are closed for the SRHT branch and for the exact rectangular-isometry signed-mixing branch; broader Gaussian/FJLT/input-sparsity routes and full downstream floating-point application/storage of a general `G` remain separate |

## Exact-Probability and Computed-Quantity Audit

The 2026-06-03 convention is part of the theorem surface: sampling
probabilities and sampling laws are exact mathematical inputs.  This exception
does not apply to any other quantity computed by an algorithm.

| Scope | Exact probability law | Computed non-probability quantities that remain charged | Current status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Algorithm 1, equations (1)--(2) | `p_ij`, the entry trace law, and the truncated/literal squared-magnitude laws are exact. | Sampled-entry rescaling, divisions by `s*p_ij`, accumulation in the sketch, hit-counter-driven stability budgets, and residual formation. | Closed for exact-law FP surfaces, source-aligned spectral corollaries, literal no-small-entry specializations, the nonconditional reciprocal-entry support-radius theorem, and the readable entry-floor specialization. The source-budget-only sharp literal equation (2) variant has a formal small-entry/source-budget obstruction and is no longer the active corrected Algorithm 1 endpoint unless a different theorem statement is explicitly requested. |
| Algorithm 2, equations (3)--(7) | Row-norm and leverage probabilities are exact laws. | Computed square-root scale denominators, actual-input sampled-row arithmetic, rounded basis-table storage for the modeled leverage endpoint, rounded row scaling, sampled sketches, optional computed Gram dot products, RHS/normal-equation data, and downstream solver inputs. | Closed for equation (5), finite-Loewner Bennett equation (7), exact-law FP transfers, the actual-input \(A=UC\) equation (7) endpoint, and the three modeled stored-basis rounded-copy equation (7) paths. |
| Algorithm 3 and uniform row sampling | Uniform row laws, Rademacher sign laws, and the SRHT/uniform-row product law are exact. | Generated/stored transform matrices, signs as stored objects, Hadamard/FHT/SRHT arithmetic, computed preconditioned bases `Vhat`, projection matrices, singular vectors or bases used to form projectors, uniform scale denominators, row scaling, sketch entries, Gram/RHS products, and solver inputs. | SRHT exact and FP transfer surfaces are closed. Generic computed signed-Hadamard/sign-storage product certificates are closed through `ComputedVector`, `ComputedMatrix.diag`, `ComputedMatrix.flProduct`, and `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamard`; exact supplied Hadamard/sign factors with rounded `H D` formation are closed through `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardExactFactors` and `signedHadamardExactFactorPreconditioner`; rounded scale/sign-pattern table certificates and their computed-left event instantiation are closed through `ComputedMatrix.flSqrtInvNatScaledPattern`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPattern`, `signedHadamardScaledPatternPreconditioner`, and `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`; concrete generated Sylvester/Walsh bit-parity sign-pattern certificates are closed through `sylvesterHadamardSignPattern`, `ComputedMatrix.flSqrtInvNatScaledSylvesterPattern`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardSylvesterPattern`, and the matching computed-left event wrappers; the concrete `fl_mul sign_i 1` stored-sign path is closed through `ComputedVector.flStoredSign`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSign`, `signedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSignPreconditioner`, and `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`; the concrete `fl_add sign_i 0` stored-sign path is closed through `ComputedVector.flStoredSignAddZeroRight`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSignAddZeroRight`, `signedHadamardScaledPatternStoredSignAddZeroRightPreconditioner`, and `signedHadamardUniformRowTraceProbability_eventProb_scaledPatternStoredSignAddZeroRightComputedLeftPreconditioned_fl_uniformRowPerturbEvent_eq_one`; the exact-input, division-then-sqrt, reciprocal-multiply-then-sqrt, split-square-root, and square-root-times-reciprocal-square-root uniform scale-denominator routines are closed through `ComputedUniformRowScaleDen.flSqrtExactInput`, `ComputedUniformRowScaleDen.flDivThenSqrt`, `ComputedUniformRowScaleDen.flInvMulThenSqrt`, `ComputedUniformRowScaleDen.flSqrtDivSqrt`, and `ComputedUniformRowScaleDen.flSqrtMulInvSqrt`; the scalar, vector-pair, propagated-input, ordered-pair-schedule, scaled-schedule, and columnwise scaled-schedule matrix FHT arithmetic adapters are closed through `flFhtButterfly_add_error_bound`, `flFhtButterfly_sub_error_bound`, `flFhtPairUpdate_error_bound`, `fhtPairUpdateExact_commute_of_disjoint`, `flFhtPairUpdate_commute_of_disjoint`, `fhtPairUpdatePropagatedErrorBudget`, `flFhtPairUpdate_propagated_error_bound`, `fhtPairSchedulePropagatedErrorBudget`, `flFhtPairSchedule_propagated_error_bound`, `fhtScaledPairScheduleErrorBudget`, `flFhtScaledPairSchedule_error_bound`, `fhtScaledPairScheduleMatrixErrorBudget`, `flFhtScaledPairScheduleMatrix_error_bound`, and `ComputedMatrix.flScaledFhtPairScheduleColumns`; the rounded sqrt-inverse FHT normalization-scale routine is closed through `fhtSqrtInvNatScale`, `flFhtSqrtInvNatScale`, `fhtSqrtInvNatScaleErrorRadius`, `flFhtSqrtInvNatScale_error_bound`, `flFhtScaledPairSchedule_sqrtInvNatScale_error_bound`, `flFhtScaledPairScheduleMatrix_sqrtInvNatScale_error_bound`, and `ComputedMatrix.flScaledFhtPairScheduleColumnsSqrtInvNat`; concrete one-stage FHT pair-list generation is closed through `fhtStagePairPredicate`, `fhtStagePairs`, `mem_fhtStagePairs_iff`, the generated-pair structural facts, `fhtSylvesterStagePairs`, and `mem_fhtSylvesterStagePairs_iff`; proof that the composed generated stages realize the Sylvester/Hadamard transform and the functional fast generated-FHT `ComputedPreconditioner` instantiation are closed through `fhtSylvesterScheduleRealizesSignPattern_generated`, `ComputedPreconditioner.flSignedHadamardSylvesterFhtSchedule`, and the matching computed-left event wrappers; scale-normalization routines beyond the rounded sqrt-inverse FHT routine if used, layout-specific in-place overwrite, vectorized-layout, or storage wrappers beyond the functional generated schedule and its same-stage no-alias/list-order/no-touch certificates, concrete SVD/QR basis routines, sign-storage formats beyond these three rounded-copy certificates, denominator normalizations beyond these five closed uniform-row paths, and non-SRHT random-transform laws are open obligations. |
| Least-squares, low-rank, matrix-completion, Laplacian applications | Any sampling laws appearing in future theorem statements are exact unless the user reopens probability-computation error. | Solver/preconditioner arithmetic, QR/SVD/projector/pseudoinverse construction, nuclear-norm/optimization solver data, graph/Laplacian/effective-resistance computations, and returned vectors or certificates. | Equation (8) sampled-row exact/FP objective transfer, concrete normal-equations solver transfer, and visible-domain stored-QR/preconditioner handoffs are closed under their stated hypotheses; the generic computed QR/preconditioner loop, random-projection LS variants, low-rank, matrix-completion, and Laplacian paper-level rows remain open. |

Algorithm 3 update: the actual-input SRHT endpoint for \(A=UC\), computed
scaled-pattern stored-sign preconditioner, concrete
`fl_mul (fl_sqrt s) (fl_div 1 (fl_sqrt m))` uniform denominator, rounded row
divisions, and rounded Gram dot products is closed in A3.2r-input.
The exact factors \(U,C\) are analysis-only; the algorithm computes with \(A\).
The finite CountSketch/input-sparsity route is now closed at the exact-law
first-moment layer and at the deterministic collision-free preservation layer:
exact hash and Rademacher signs give \(\mathbb E[(SU)^T(SU)] = U^T U\), and an
injective exact hash with signs satisfying \(\omega_k^2=1\) gives exact
Gram preservation.  The exact pair-collision law is also closed:
for distinct input rows \(a\ne b\), \(\Pr[h(a)=h(b)]=1/r\) and
\(\Pr[h(a)\ne h(b)]=1-1/r\).  The exact global hash-injectivity event is now
closed by a union bound:
\(\Pr[h\hbox{ injective}]\ge 1-\sum_{a\ne b}1/r\ge 1-m^2/r\).  This is
non-vacuous when \(r>m^2\) and is only a collision-free sufficient route;
the per-entry sparse floating-point apply kernel is also closed for exact
hash/sign data: if \(b_i=|\{k:h(k)=i\}|\), the rounded signed products and
left-to-right bucket accumulation have componentwise radius
\((u+\gamma_{b_i}+u\gamma_{b_i})\sum_{k:h(k)=i}|\omega_k||A_{kj}|\), and
under exact sign magnitude control this is bounded by
\((u+\gamma_{b_i}+u\gamma_{b_i})\|A_{:j}\|_1\).  This computed-entry result
is now packaged as the computed sparse matrix
\(\widehat Y=\operatorname{fl}(S_{h,\omega}A)\), with the same bound proved
for every output entry.  The rounded sparse Gram arithmetic is also closed:
Lean defines \(\widehat G=\operatorname{fl}(\widehat Y^T\widehat Y)\) by
length-\(r\) floating-point dot products and proves a Frobenius perturbation
bound against the exact \(G=(S_{h,\omega}A)^T(S_{h,\omega}A)\) using only the
explicit sparse-apply radius and \(\gamma_r\).  Later rows now compose the
sparse transform with downstream uniform row sampling in collision-free and
non-injective Markov/Frobenius finite-Loewner forms, including the concrete
`fl_mul (fl_sqrt s) (fl_div 1 (fl_sqrt r))` denominator path.  What remains
open is the sharper CountSketch concentration/subspace-embedding theory,
tree/parallel reassociation or aliasing sparse-sketch memory semantics beyond
fixed per-bucket permutations, and non-CountSketch Gaussian/FJLT/input-sparsity
transform laws.

| Algorithm 3 CountSketch non-injective moment foundation | Exact sign/hash moments for standard CountSketch variance route | `FiniteProbability.expectationReal_indicator_eq_eventProb`, `countSketchHashProbability_expectationReal_pairCollisionIndicator_eq_inv`, `countSketchProbability_expectationReal_pairCollisionIndicator_eq_inv`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_eq_zero_of_flip_neg`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_four_eq_zero_of_left_unpaired`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_four_eq_one_same_pair`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_four_eq_one_reversed_pair`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sign_pair_mul_sign_pair_eq`, `rademacherTraceProbability_expectationReal_sq_sum_distinctPair_mul_sign_pair_eq` | Closed as exact-probability/exact-arithmetic infrastructure. The Rademacher flip-symmetry classifier proves that two ordered distinct sign pairs have nonzero fourth moment only when the pairs are identical or reversed; the summed theorem packages this for arbitrary coefficients \(c_{ab}\). This is the next local foundation for a non-collision-free CountSketch Frobenius/Loewner concentration theorem, not yet that theorem or a floating-point endpoint. |
| Algorithm 3 CountSketch fixed-vector quadratic-form moment foundation | Exact one-vector CountSketch Gram-error moment and tail | `finiteQuadraticForm_rowGram_eq_vecNorm2Sq_rectMatMulVec`, `rowGram_singleton_col_eq_vecNorm2Sq`, `finiteQuadraticForm_rowGram_eq_rowGram_rectMatMulVec_singleton`, `rectMatMulVec_preconditionRows_eq_preconditionRows_rectMatMulVec`, `finiteQuadraticForm_rowGram_preconditionRows_eq_rowGram_preconditionRows_rectMatMulVec_singleton`, `finiteQuadraticForm_rowGram_preconditionRows_sub_rowGram_eq_rowGram_singleton_error`, `countSketchProbability_expectationReal_rowGram_quadratic_error_sq_le`, `countSketchAllPairs_vecCoeffSq_sum_eq_vecNorm2Sq_sq`, `countSketchDistinctPair_vecCoeffSq_sum_le_vecNorm2Sq_sq`, `countSketchProbability_expectationReal_rowGram_quadratic_error_sq_le_vecNorm`, `countSketchProbability_expectationReal_rowGram_quadratic_error_sq_le_frobNorm`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub_delta_of_coeff_budget` | Closed as exact-law/exact-arithmetic infrastructure for the non-injective route. For exact \(A\) and fixed exact vector \(x\), Lean proves that \(x^T((SA)^T(SA)-A^TA)x\) is the one-column CountSketch Gram-entry error for \(Ax\). Hence \(E[(x^T\Delta x)^2]\le 2r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((Ax)_a(Ax)_b)^2\), with readable bounds \(2\|Ax\|_2^4/r\) and \(2\|A\|_F^4\|x\|_2^4/r\). The tail wrapper gives probability at least \(1-\delta\) if \(2r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((Ax)_a(Ax)_b)^2/\eta^2\le\delta\). The irreducible order is \(\Theta(r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((Ax)_a(Ax)_b)^2/\eta^2)\), with readable \(O(\|Ax\|_2^4/(r\eta^2))\). This is not a floating-point theorem and not a uniform-over-all-vectors CountSketch embedding theorem. |
| Algorithm 3 CountSketch finite-test quadratic-form probability foundation | Exact finite-family CountSketch Gram-error event | `countSketchProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub_sum_coeff_budget`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub_delta_of_sum_coeff_budget`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub_sum_vecNorm_budget`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_rowGram_quadratic_error_le_ge_one_sub_sum_frobNorm_budget` | Closed as the exact finite-union wrapper around the fixed-vector theorem. For a finite exact test set \(x_\alpha\) and \(\eta_\alpha>0\), Lean proves probability at least \(1-\sum_\alpha 2r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((A x_\alpha)_a(A x_\alpha)_b)^2/\eta_\alpha^2\) for the simultaneous event \(\forall\alpha,\ |x_\alpha^T\Delta x_\alpha|\le\eta_\alpha\), plus the direct \(1-\delta\) wrapper when this displayed loss is at most \(\delta\). The readable losses replace the coefficient sum by \(\|A x_\alpha\|_2^4\) or \(\|A\|_F^4\|x_\alpha\|_2^4\). The irreducible order is \(\Theta(\sum_\alpha r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((A x_\alpha)_a(A x_\alpha)_b)^2/\eta_\alpha^2)\) when nonzero. This is exact-law infrastructure, not an FP endpoint or a uniform embedding theorem by itself. |
| Algorithm 3 CountSketch finite-cover exact Loewner probability foundation | Exact finite-cover CountSketch two-sided Loewner event | `countSketchRowGramFiniteTestEvent`, `countSketchRowGramTwoSidedLoewnerCoverEvent`, `countSketchRowGramFiniteTestFrobEvent_subset_twoSidedLoewnerCoverEvent`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_rowGram_twoSidedLoewner_cover_ge_one_sub_sum_coeff_add_frob`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_rowGram_twoSidedLoewner_cover_ge_one_sub_delta_of_sum_coeff_add_frob_budget`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_rowGram_twoSidedLoewner_cover_ge_one_sub_sum_vecNorm_add_frobNorm`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_rowGram_twoSidedLoewner_cover_ge_one_sub_delta_of_sum_vecNorm_add_frobNorm_budget` | Closed as an exact-law finite-cover upgrade with no supplied operator certificate. The proof intersects the finite-test event with \(\|\Delta\|_F\le L\), so the Frobenius event supplies the cover theorem's coarse operator radius. The readable Lean wrapper gives the sufficient loss \(\sum_\alpha2\|Az_\alpha\|_2^4/(r\eta^2)+2\|A\|_F^4/(rL^2)\) and its own target-budget theorem. The irreducible order is \(\Theta(\sum_\alpha r^{-1}\sum_{a\ne b}((Az_\alpha)_a(Az_\alpha)_b)^2/\eta^2+r^{-1}\sum_{j,\ell,a\ne b}(A_{aj}A_{b\ell})^2/L^2)\), with readable \(O(\sum_\alpha\|Az_\alpha\|_2^4/(r\eta^2)+\|A\|_F^4/(rL^2))\). It is not the standard CountSketch concentration/subspace-embedding theorem and does not close row-sampling composition after CountSketch or the complete input-sparsity Algorithm 3 theorem. |
| Algorithm 3 CountSketch full-law collision-free sparse Gram FP endpoint | Exact hash-sign probability plus deterministic sparse Gram FP arithmetic | `countSketchProbability`, `countSketchFlSparseGramDotRowGramPerturbEvent`, `countSketchProbability_injectiveFst_subset_flSparseGramDot_rowGram_perturbEvent`, `countSketchProbability_eventProb_flSparseGramDot_rowGram_perturb_ge_one_sub_square_inv` | Closed for the fully realized collision-free sufficient route: under the exact `countSketchProbability` law over \(x=(h,\Omega)\), with \(\omega=\operatorname{rademacherSignVector}(\Omega)\), the computed sparse Gram event \(\|\widehat G(h,\Omega)-A^TA\|_F\le T_{\rm CSGram}^{\rm fp}(h,\omega)\) has probability at least \(1-m^2/r\). The append/flatMap lemmas prove that the flat exact schedule, flat rounded schedule, and flat propagated budget are definitionally the stage-by-stage recurrences over `List.range p`; the stage-list append and `List.range (stage+1)` lemmas additionally prove that exact schedules, rounded schedules, and propagated budgets advance by applying all previous stages followed by the current stage. ## External Literature Notes

The 2026-05-25 continuation pass searched external primary literature for the
matrix-concentration gap after the local repository and bundled mathlib search
found no finite matrix Bernstein/Khintchine theorem.

| Source | Exact location | Role | Local status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Live proof-source ledger | `docs/RANDNLA_CACM_PROOF_SOURCE_LEDGER.md` | Front-loaded source-chain map for every incomplete CACM proof and open paper-level row | Updated on 2026-05-27; advisory until cited results are formalized locally |
| Joel A. Tropp, "User-friendly tail bounds for sums of random matrices," Found. Comput. Math. 12 (2012), 389--434, arXiv:1004.4389, https://arxiv.org/abs/1004.4389 | Theorem 3.2, Lieb's concavity theorem for `A ↦ tr exp(H + log A)` | Exact missing concavity theorem behind the chosen trace-MGF route | Advisory only; not formalized and not used as a Lean hypothesis |
| Same Tropp source | Corollary 3.3, one-step trace expectation inequality from Lieb plus Jensen | Source statement for the next trace-MGF theorem after the finite Jensen adapter | Advisory only; finite Jensen is formalized locally, but Lieb concavity and the one-step trace-MGF theorem are not |
| Joel A. Tropp, "User-friendly tail bounds for sums of random matrices," Found. Comput. Math. 12 (2012), 389--434, arXiv:1004.4389, https://arxiv.org/abs/1004.4389 | Theorem 3.6, master tail bound for independent self-adjoint sums | Source-chain target for a future trace-exponential/largest-eigenvalue proof of A1.5 and matrix Bernstein | Advisory only; not formalized and not used as a Lean hypothesis |
| Same Tropp source | Corollary 3.7, trace-exponential tail bound from an MGF/CGF domination hypothesis | Identifies the missing trace-exponential/matrix-log step after the repository's scalar exponential Markov kernel | Advisory only; local proof not yet present |
| Same Tropp source | Theorem 1.4, Matrix Bernstein for self-adjoint sums | Candidate reusable theorem shape for A1.5 once finite self-adjoint spectral infrastructure exists | Advisory only; local proof not yet present |
| Same Tropp source | Theorem 1.6, rectangular matrix Bernstein via self-adjoint dilation | Confirms that the repository's self-adjoint dilation route is the right formal target for rectangular Algorithm 1 residuals | Advisory only; local proof not yet present |
| Joel A. Tropp, "An Introduction to Matrix Concentration Inequalities," Found. Trends Mach. Learn. 8(1--2), 2015, preprint https://tropp.caltech.edu/books/Tro14-Introduction-Matrix-preprint.pdf | Theorem 8.1.1, Lieb's concavity theorem and relative-entropy proof route; Proposition 8.3.5 generalized Klein; Theorem 8.1.4 joint convexity | Supporting primary/monograph source for formalizing the Lieb concavity foundation | Advisory source; local scalar/vector relative-entropy nonnegativity, finite log-sum inequality, scalar/vector/real-diagonal joint-convexity subcases, C-star relative-entropy vocabulary, diagonal/scalar-identity cases, generalized Klein, matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity, and the normalized variational formula are now formalized. The noncommutative matrix relative-entropy joint-convexity foundation remains open. |
| Edward G. Effros, "A Matrix Convexity Approach to Some Celebrated Quantum Inequalities," Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 106(4), 1006--1008, 2009, arXiv:0802.1234, https://arxiv.org/abs/0802.1234 | Matrix perspective route for quantum relative-entropy joint convexity | Primary source candidate for proving `cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive` from finite-dimensional operator-convex perspective machinery | Advisory only; no theorem from this paper is used as a Lean hypothesis |
| Göran Lindblad, "Completely positive maps and entropy inequalities," Communications in Mathematical Physics 40, 147--151, 1975, DOI https://doi.org/10.1007/BF01609396 | Original joint-convexity/monotonicity lineage for quantum relative entropy | Historical primary source for the theorem currently blocking the Tropp route | Advisory only; the next Lean route remains the finite Effros/Tropp matrix-perspective proof |
| Frank Hansen and Gert K. Pedersen, "Jensen's Operator Inequality," Bulletin of the London Mathematical Society 35(4), 553--564, 2003, arXiv:math/0204049, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0204049 | Operator Jensen inequality for noncommutative convex combinations | Source foundation behind Effros's matrix perspective theorem for operator-convex functions | Advisory only; no operator Jensen theorem is used as a Lean hypothesis |
| Julius Bendat and Seymour Sherman, "Monotone and convex operator functions," Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 79 (1955), 58--71, DOI https://doi.org/10.1090/S0002-9947-1955-0082655-4 | Convexity/monotonicity characterization through divided differences | Advisory alternate source for proving `cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget` | Advisory only; no divided-difference theorem is used as a Lean hypothesis |

| Drineas--Zouzias, "A Note on Element-wise Matrix Sparsification via a Matrix-valued Bernstein Inequality," arXiv:1006.0407, https://arxiv.org/pdf/1006.0407 | Algorithm 1 and Theorem 1, truncated matrix `\hat A` before square-proportional sampling | Source-alignment target for a hard-thresholded elementwise spectral route | Closed for the truncated square source-variant theorem: deterministic truncation, exact source sample-budget theorem, and support-aware FP gamma-budget transfer are formalized locally; this does not close the literal untruncated CACM Algorithm 1 law |

## Historical Bottleneck Ledger: A1.5-B1 Closed

There is currently no active red bottleneck for the cited square
source-aligned Algorithm 1 theorem.  The table below records the historical
A1.5-B1 route and the dependency closures that led to the final exact and FP
source-budget theorems.  Future full-paper work should choose a new open row
from the not-proved ledger rather than continuing to orbit A1.5-B1.

| Bottleneck | Source claim | Blocking Lean theorem | Required dependencies | Closed in this pass | Failed or rejected routes | Next dependency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1.5-B1 | CACM Algorithm 1 equation (2), spectral-norm concentration for squared-magnitude elementwise sampling | A future finite self-adjoint trace-exponential/largest-eigenvalue theorem that supplies `algorithm1ExactDilationEigenUpperEvent` or `algorithm1ExactDilationUpperEvent` with high probability, then transfers to `algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent` and the FP event. | Product-law zero-mean and variance proxies; bounded-increment and truncated support prerequisites; local finite PSD/Loewner/spectral bridges; scalar/diagonal matrix-exponential normalization; trace diagonalization for the matrix exponential; positive, negative, and two-sided MGF-to-eigenvalue tail interfaces; finite-real to complex C⋆ embedding/order bridge; strict-positive regularization and regularized operator-log bridge; complex C⋆ trace bridge including cyclicity and spectral-order trace positivity; complex C⋆ expectation/order bridge; finite Jensen adapter; Lieb trace concavity; trace-exponential domination; Bernstein/Khintchine instantiation. | `finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_eq_sum_exp_finiteHermitianEigenvalues` and `finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_neg_eq_sum_exp_neg_finiteHermitianEigenvalues` close positive and negative power-series matrix-exponential trace diagonalization. The upper-tail theorems `FiniteProbability.eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_ge_le_exp_neg_mul_expected_trace_exp`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_ge_le_exp_neg_mul_trace_bound`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_lt_ge_one_sub_exp_neg_mul_expected_trace_exp`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_lt_ge_one_sub_exp_neg_mul_trace_bound`, lower-tail companions `FiniteProbability.eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_le_le_exp_mul_expected_trace_exp_neg`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_le_le_exp_mul_trace_bound`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_gt_ge_one_sub_exp_mul_expected_trace_exp_neg`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_gt_ge_one_sub_exp_mul_trace_bound`, and two-sided combiners `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_lt_ge_one_sub_exp_neg_mul_expected_trace_exp_add`, `FiniteProbability.eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_lt_ge_one_sub_exp_neg_mul_trace_bound_add` close the MGF-to-eigenvalue tail/complement interfaces. The bounded-increment prerequisite now also has `finiteLoewnerLe_smul_id_of_finiteOpNorm2Le`, `finiteLoewnerLe_neg_smul_id_of_finiteOpNorm2Le`, `truncatedDilationIncrementLoewnerBoundedEvent`, and `sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_truncatedDilationIncrementLoewnerBoundedEvent_eq_one`, so retained dilation increments have probability-one two-sided Loewner bounds, not just operator/squared bounds. A1.5aa adds `finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_le_card_mul_exp_of_finiteLoewnerLe_smul_id` and its negative companion, closing the scalar matrix-CGF-to-trace-exponential bound once a future noncommutative trace-MGF theorem supplies a scalar-identity Loewner CGF estimate. A1.5ab adds the weak accumulated bounded-increment theorem `truncatedDilationIncrementLoewnerBoundedEvent_subset_exactDilationUpperEvent_sum_bound` and its probability-one exact dilation/spectral consequences, confirming that the bounded-increment event composes correctly while still paying the worst-case `sL` scale. A1.5ac adds `cstarMatrix_log_le_log`, a repository-local wrapper around mathlib operator-log monotonicity for complex `CStarMatrix`. A1.5ad adds `finiteComplexCStarMatrix` and preservation of entries, subtraction, scalar identities, self-adjointness, local PSD, and local Loewner order into complex `CStarMatrix`. A1.5ae adds positive scalar-identity regularization and the finite-real regularized operator-log inequality. A1.5af adds `cstarMatrixTrace`, finite-real trace agreement, real-part PSD/Loewner trace transfer, scalar CFC-exponential trace normalization, cyclicity `cstarMatrixTrace_mul_comm`, and spectral-order trace real-part positivity/monotonicity via `cstarMatrixTrace_re_nonneg_of_nonneg` and `cstarMatrixTrace_re_mono`, plus self-adjoint trace real-valuedness via `cstarMatrixTrace_im_eq_zero_of_isSelfAdjoint`. A1.5ag adds C⋆-matrix expectation, complex and real weighted-sum forms, trace-expectation interchange, nonnegativity preservation, monotonicity, and strict-positive expectation regularization. A1.5ah adds the finite Jensen adapter for concave real-valued functions of C⋆-matrix random variables. A1.5ak adds local `log(exp X)=X` wrappers, A1.5al adds local `exp(log A)=A` wrappers for finite complex C⋆ matrices, and A1.5am identifies the local CFC Lieb functional with the normed-algebra exponential form while normalizing the `H = 0` case. A1.5au adds the real diagonal matrix relative-entropy reduction and nonnegativity case, A1.5av adds the conditional reduction from matrix relative-entropy joint convexity plus the normalized variational formula to local Lieb trace concavity, A1.5aw proves the optimizer-candidate equality part of the normalized variational formula, A1.5ax reduces the full normalized variational formula to matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity on the strictly positive cone, and A1.5ay reduces that nonnegativity target to the generalized Klein first-order trace inequality for the entropy trace functional. These close the algebraic/order/regularized-log/trace/expectation/Jensen, local trace-exponential normalization, commutative relative-entropy sanity bridges, and the conditional Tropp route reductions. A1.5bc also closes the generalized Klein first-order trace inequality, local matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity, and the normalized entropy variational formula, leaving matrix relative-entropy joint convexity, trace-MGF domination, and matrix Bernstein open. | Local/library search found no ready finite-probability matrix Bernstein/Khintchine theorem. The accumulated `sL` route was recorded as a weak theorem only because it ignores zero mean and variance and cannot recover equation (2). A generic CFC equality route was not used because the repository-native proof can diagonalize the actual power-series `finiteMatrixExp` directly while keeping matrix-ring instances explicit. | Prove `cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`, then use A1.5bc, A1.5av and the finite Jensen adapter to prove the one-step trace-MGF domination theorem and instantiate the positive and negative trace-MGF controls with the Algorithm 1 dilation residual. |

A1.5-B1 bottleneck table addendum: A1.5an now also proves
`liebTraceConcavityTarget_zero`, the affine `H = 0` special case of the local
Lieb trace-concavity target. The arbitrary self-adjoint-`H` Lieb theorem
remains the next true bottleneck dependency.

A1.5-B1 bottleneck table addendum: A1.5ao now proves
`FiniteProbability.exists_prob_pos` and
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_isStrictlyPositive`, closing the
strict-positive finite-expectation domain lemma needed before a future theorem
can form `log(E[exp X])`. It does not by itself prove strict positivity of
matrix exponentials; that separate dependency is closed by A1.5ap. It does not
prove the Lieb/Tropp trace-MGF inequality.

A1.5-B1 bottleneck table addendum: A1.5ap now proves
`cstarMatrix_normedSpace_exp_isStrictlyPositive_of_isSelfAdjoint`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_complex_exp_isStrictlyPositive_of_isSelfAdjoint`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_isStrictlyPositive_of_isSelfAdjoint`, and
`liebTraceCfcExp_isStrictlyPositive`, closing the strict positivity of
self-adjoint finite complex C-star matrix exponentials. This supplies the
domain fact needed before a future theorem can combine A1.5ao with
`log(E[exp X])`. It does not prove arbitrary-`H` Lieb trace concavity,
trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 update: A1.5ag closes the complex/C⋆ finite-probability expectation
and order dependency through `FiniteProbability.expectationComplex`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix`,
`FiniteProbability.cstarMatrixTrace_expectationCStarMatrix`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_finiteComplexCStarMatrix`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationComplex_re`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_eq_sum_real_smul`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationReal_le_of_concaveOn`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationReal_le_of_concaveOn_expectationCStarMatrix`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_nonneg`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_mono`, and
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_add_pos_smul_one_isStrictlyPositive`.
The next missing dependency remains the actual Lieb trace-concavity theorem for
the map `A ↦ tr exp(H + log A)`, which is the mathematical engine behind the
one-step noncommutative Tropp trace-MGF domination theorem.

A1.5-B1 domain update: A1.5aj adds `LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean`
with `strictPositiveCStarMatrixCone`, scalar positivity lemmas,
`strictPositiveCStarMatrixCone_convex`, `cstarMatrix_log_isSelfAdjoint`,
`liebTraceArgument_isSelfAdjoint`, `liebTraceArgument_isStarNormal`, `liebTraceCfcExp_nonneg`,
`liebTraceFunctional_trace_im_eq_zero`, `liebTraceFunctional`,
`liebTraceFunctional_nonneg`, and `liebTraceConcavityTarget`. This closes the
strictly-positive-domain convexity, self-adjoint/star-normal argument
well-formedness, local target-statement vocabulary, trace-exponential
nonnegativity, trace real-valuedness, and nonnegativity of the local Lieb
functional listed in the proof-source queue. It does not prove Lieb trace
concavity or the one-step trace-MGF theorem.

A1.5-B1 analytic update: A1.5ak extends
`LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/OperatorLog.lean` with the finite-dimensional
`CStarMatrix` bridges `cstarMatrix_normedSpaceExp_isTopologicalRing` and
`cstarMatrix_normedRingExp_isTopologicalRing`, the real self-adjoint calculus
bridge `cstarMatrix_realContinuousFunctionalCalculus`, and the
log-exponential identities `cstarMatrix_log_normedSpace_exp_of_isSelfAdjoint`,
`cstarMatrix_log_cfc_complex_exp_of_isSelfAdjoint`, and
`cstarMatrix_log_cfc_real_exp_of_isSelfAdjoint`. This closes the listed
`log(exp X)=X` prerequisite for self-adjoint matrices in the Tropp/Lieb route.
It does not prove Lieb trace concavity, the one-step trace-MGF theorem, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or CACM equation (2).

A1.5-B1 analytic update: A1.5al extends
`LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/OperatorLog.lean` with the nonnegative-spectrum
bridge `cstarMatrix_normedRingExp_nonnegSpectrumClass` and the strictly
positive inverse identities
`cstarMatrix_normedSpace_exp_log_of_isStrictlyPositive`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_complex_exp_log_of_isStrictlyPositive`, and
`cstarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_log_of_isStrictlyPositive`. This closes the listed
local `exp(log A)=A` wrapper dependency for strictly positive finite complex
C-star matrices in the Tropp/Lieb route. It does not prove Lieb trace
concavity, the one-step trace-MGF theorem, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or
CACM equation (2).

A1.5-B1 analytic update: A1.5am extends
`LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Analysis/LiebTrace.lean` with
`liebTraceFunctional_eq_normedSpace_exp`, identifying the local CFC form of
the Lieb functional with the standard normed-algebra exponential
`Re tr(exp(H + log A))`, and `liebTraceFunctional_zero_eq_trace`, normalizing
the functional at `H = 0` on the strictly positive cone. These close
trace-exponential normalization dependencies for the Tropp/Lieb route only.
They do not prove Lieb trace concavity, the one-step trace-MGF theorem, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or CACM equation (2).

A1.5-B1 convex-analysis update: A1.5an proves
`liebTraceConcavityTarget_zero`, the `H = 0` special case of the local Lieb
trace-concavity target. The proof uses `liebTraceFunctional_zero_eq_trace` to
reduce the functional to `A ↦ Re tr(A)`, then trace additivity/scaling and
convexity of the strictly positive cone. It does not prove the arbitrary
self-adjoint-`H` Lieb theorem needed by the Tropp one-step trace-MGF theorem,
matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or CACM equation (2).

A1.5-B1 relative-entropy update: A1.5ar adds
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy` and proves `cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_self`,
the diagonal normalization for the relative-entropy expression used in the
chosen Tropp monograph route to Lieb concavity. This is a listed route
dependency, not a proof of matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity, joint
convexity, the variational principle, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or CACM equation (2).

A1.5-B1 scalar/vector entropy update: A1.5as adds
`realRelativeEntropy`, `realRelativeEntropy_nonneg`,
`finiteRealRelativeEntropy`, and `finiteRealRelativeEntropy_nonneg`, proving
the scalar positive-input inequality from `log x <= x - 1` and summing it for
finite positive vectors. This is a commutative foundation for the chosen route,
not the noncommutative matrix relative-entropy theorem needed to derive Lieb
trace concavity.

A1.5-B1 scalar-identity matrix entropy update: A1.5at adds
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_algebraMap_real` and
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_algebraMap_real_nonneg`, proving that the C-star
matrix relative-entropy expression reduces to dimension times scalar relative
entropy on real scalar identities and is therefore nonnegative there. This is
still only a scalar-identity matrix case, not general matrix relative-entropy
nonnegativity, joint convexity, the variational principle, Lieb concavity, or
trace-MGF domination.

A1.5-B1 diagonal matrix entropy update: A1.5au adds
`cstarMatrixDiagonalStarAlgHom`, `cstarMatrixDiagonalStarAlgHom_continuous`,
`cstarMatrixRealDiagonal`, `cstarMatrixTrace_realDiagonal`,
`cstarMatrix_log_realDiagonal`,
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_realDiagonal`, and
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_realDiagonal_nonneg`, proving that the C-star
relative-entropy expression reduces to the finite-vector relative entropy on
real diagonal matrices and is therefore nonnegative for positive diagonal
entries.  This is still the commutative diagonal case, not general
noncommutative matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity, joint convexity, the
variational principle, Lieb concavity, or trace-MGF domination.

A1.5-B1 relative-entropy route update: A1.5av adds
`cstarMatrixEntropyVariationalObjective`,
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`,
`cstarMatrixEntropyVariationalFormula`, and
`liebTraceConcavityTarget_of_relativeEntropy_route`.  It proves the local
Tropp route reduction with the corrected normalization: the variational
objective contains the extra `Re tr A` term corresponding to
`D(X;A)=Re tr(X(log X-log A)-(X-A))`.  Joint convexity of matrix relative
entropy on the strictly positive cone plus this normalized entropy
variational formula imply the local Lieb trace-concavity target.  The
joint-convexity and variational-formula hypotheses are explicit foundations;
A1.5bc now closes the variational-formula side; at that stage joint convexity
was the open relative-entropy foundation.  Later rows close joint
convexity and the resulting unconditional Lieb theorem.

A1.5-B1 variational-formula substep: A1.5aw adds
`cstarMatrixEntropyVariationalObjective_liebOptimizer`, proving that the
candidate \(X_\star=\exp(H+\log A)\) has the correct objective value for the
normalized variational formula.  This closes only the attainment/equality
algebra; A1.5bc supplies the global upper-bound part through generalized Klein
and relative-entropy nonnegativity.

A1.5-B1 variational-formula reduction: A1.5ax adds
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyNonnegOnStrictPositive` and
`cstarMatrixEntropyVariationalFormula_of_relativeEntropy_nonneg`, proving that
the normalized variational formula follows from nonnegativity of matrix
relative entropy on the strictly positive cone.  A1.5bc now supplies that
nonnegativity input.  At that stage the open relative-entropy foundation
was joint convexity; later rows close it.

A1.5-B1 nonnegativity-source reduction: A1.5ay adds
`cstarMatrixEntropyTraceFirstOrderConvexityOnStrictPositive` and
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyNonnegOnStrictPositive_of_entropyTraceFirstOrder`.
This proves that the generalized Klein first-order trace inequality for
\(\Phi(X)=\operatorname{Re}\operatorname{tr}(X\log X-X)\) implies local
matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity.  The nonnegativity frontier is now the
source-aligned first-order trace inequality itself; A1.5bc closes that
frontier. Joint convexity remains the separate
operator-perspective/Kronecker-product foundation.

A1.5-B1 generalized-Klein closure: A1.5bc adds
`matrixTrace_hermitianCfc_entropy_firstOrder_compact_nonneg`,
`cstarMatrix_nonneg_to_matrix_posSemidef`,
`cstarMatrix_isStrictlyPositive_to_matrix_posDef`,
`cstarMatrixEntropyTraceFirstOrderConvexityOnStrictPositive_of_hermitianCfc`,
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyNonnegOnStrictPositive_of_hermitianCfc`,
`cstarMatrixEntropyVariationalFormula_of_hermitianCfc`, and
`liebTraceConcavityTarget_of_relativeEntropy_jointConvex`. This closes the
compact Hermitian/C-star generalized Klein bridge, local relative-entropy
nonnegativity, and the normalized variational formula. It does not prove
relative-entropy joint convexity, nonconditional trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 commutative joint-convexity update: A1.5bd adds
`finite_log_sum_inequality`,
`realRelativeEntropy_jointConvex_of_pos_weights`,
`realRelativeEntropy_jointConvex`,
`finiteRealRelativeEntropy_jointConvex`,
`cstarMatrixRealDiagonal_smul_add`, `positive_weighted_sum_pos`, and
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropy_realDiagonal_jointConvex`. This closes the
finite log-sum theorem, scalar/vector relative-entropy joint convexity, and
the real-diagonal C-star matrix joint-convexity subcase. It does not prove
the noncommutative strict-cone joint-convexity theorem
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`, arbitrary
self-adjoint-`H` Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 operator-perspective substrate update: A1.5be adds
`cstarMatrixLeftMul`, `cstarMatrixRightMul`,
`cstarMatrixLeftMul_real_smul_add`,
`cstarMatrixRightMul_real_smul_add`,
`cstarMatrixLeftRightMul_commute`,
`cstarMatrixLeftMul_isUnit_of_isStrictlyPositive`, and
`cstarMatrixRightMul_isUnit_of_isStrictlyPositive`, together with the unit
inverse multiplication lemmas. This closes the algebraic left/right
multiplication layer needed to state the finite-dimensional Effros
perspective operator \(L_X R_A^{-1}\). It does not prove operator convexity,
the perspective theorem, noncommutative relative-entropy joint convexity,
Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or
equation (2).

A1.5-B1 ratio-operator update: A1.5bf adds
`cstarMatrixLeftRightRatio`, `cstarMatrixLeftRightRatio_apply`,
`cstarMatrixLeftRightRatio_apply_unit`,
`cstarMatrixLeftRightRatio_apply_of_unit_eq`, and
`cstarMatrixLeftRightRatio_apply_of_isStrictlyPositive`. This closes the
explicit \(L_X R_A^{-1}\) endomorphism and its base-point normalization
\((L_XR_A^{-1})(A)=X\) for unit, hence strictly positive, \(A\). It does
not prove operator convexity, the Effros perspective theorem, the trace
representation of matrix relative entropy, noncommutative joint convexity,
Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or
equation (2).

A1.5-B1 product/power update: A1.5bg adds `cstarMatrixLeftMul_mul`,
`cstarMatrixRightMul_mul`, `cstarMatrixLeftMul_pow`, and
`cstarMatrixRightMul_pow`. This closes the product and natural-power algebra
for \(L_A\) and \(R_A\), needed before a polynomial/continuous functional
calculus trace-representation argument for \(L_XR_A^{-1}\). It does not prove
operator convexity, the Effros perspective theorem, the trace representation
itself, noncommutative joint convexity, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination,
matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 Kronecker update: A1.5bh adds
`matrix_kronecker_left_identity_real_smul_add`,
`matrix_kronecker_right_identity_real_smul_add`,
`matrix_kronecker_left_identity_mul_right_identity`,
`matrix_kronecker_right_identity_mul_left_identity`,
`matrix_kronecker_left_right_commute`,
`matrix_kronecker_posDef_left_identity`, and
`matrix_kronecker_posDef_right_identity`. This closes the finite Kronecker
lift substrate \(A\mapsto A\otimes I\) and \(H\mapsto I\otimes H\) used in
Tropp/Effros's operator-perspective setup: the lifts are affine for real
weighted sums, commute, multiply to \(A\otimes H\), and preserve positive
definiteness. It does not prove operator convexity, the Effros perspective
theorem, a trace representation for matrix relative entropy, noncommutative
joint convexity, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 Kronecker trace update: A1.5bi adds `matrix_trace_kronecker`,
`matrix_trace_kronecker_left_identity`, and
`matrix_trace_kronecker_right_identity`. This closes the finite trace algebra
for the Kronecker lifts: \(\operatorname{tr}(A\otimes H)=
\operatorname{tr}(A)\operatorname{tr}(H)\) and the identity-lift traces are
\(d\,\operatorname{tr}(A)\). It does not prove operator convexity, the
Effros perspective theorem, the relative-entropy trace representation itself,
noncommutative joint convexity, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 Hansen--Pedersen target update: A1.5bj adds
`cstarMatrixHansenPedersenJensenTwoPointTarget`,
`cstarMatrixHansenPedersenJensenTwoPointTarget_id`, and
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget`. This locks the next source
theorem to the finite two-point Hansen--Pedersen Jensen inequality and proves
only the identity-function sanity case. It does not prove nonlinear operator
Jensen, operator convexity of \(x\log x\), the Effros perspective theorem, the
relative-entropy trace representation, noncommutative joint convexity, Lieb
concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2).

A1.5-B1 Hansen--Pedersen source-route split update: A1.5bk separates the
source route into `cstarMatrixPositiveOperatorConvexTarget`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveHansenPedersenTransferTarget`, and the concrete
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget` /
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenTransferTarget` pair.  It also proves
`cstarMatrixPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_id` as the identity-function sanity
case for ordinary matrix convexity.  This corrects the dependency graph:
operator convexity of \(x\log x\) and the Hansen--Pedersen transfer theorem
are distinct open dependencies before the assembled
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget` can close.

A1.5-B1 Hansen--Pedersen assembly update: A1.5bl proves
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_positiveOperatorConvex_of_transfer`.
This adapter closes the listed assembly dependency: once the concrete
\(x\log x\) operator-convexity target and the concrete transfer target close,
the assembled two-point Jensen target follows without an extra hypothesis.
It does not prove either of those two nonlinear inputs.

A1.5-B1 transfer-only bridge update: A1.5cg proves
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_transfer`. Since A1.5cf closes
the ordinary \(x\log x\) positive-cone operator-convexity target, the concrete
Hansen--Pedersen Jensen target is now blocked only by
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenTransferTarget`, not by any remaining
ordinary operator-convexity theorem.

A1.5-B1 all-finite source correction: A1.5ch observes that the standard
Hansen--Pedersen block proof uses a larger finite matrix algebra, so the
source-faithful transfer theorem is the all-finite-size target
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenTransferAllFiniteTarget`. The new theorem
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexAllFiniteTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`
packages A1.5cf across all finite index types, and
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_allFiniteTransfer` reduces the
assembled Jensen target to that all-finite transfer theorem.

A1.5-B1 block-substrate update: A1.5ci closes the block-column,
block-diagonal star-algebra, and block-order substrate used by the
Hansen--Pedersen proof.  The shared bridge definitions
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal` and `cstarMatrixColumnPair` represent
\(\operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2)\) and \(V=[A;B]\).  Theorems
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_conjTranspose_mul_self`,
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_mul_columnPair`,
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_conjTranspose_mul_blockDiagonal_mul_columnPair`, and
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_conjTranspose_mul_self_eq_one_of_sum` prove
\[
  V^*V=A^*A+B^*B,\qquad
  \operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2)V=[T_1A;T_2B],\qquad
  V^*\operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2)V=A^*T_1A+B^*T_2B,
\]
and the isometry normalization from \(A^*A+B^*B=I\).  Theorems
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_star`, `cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_mul`,
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_isUnit`, `cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_nonneg`, and
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_isStrictlyPositive` also show that block diagonals
preserve the star-algebra operations and the positive/strictly-positive cone.
This is a genuine dependency closure for the all-finite Hansen--Pedersen route,
but it does not prove the CFC/Jensen step for nonlinear operator-convex
functions.

A1.5-B1 block-diagonal CFC update: A1.5cj closes the functional-calculus
decomposition for block diagonals.  For self-adjoint \(T_1,T_2\) and \(f\)
continuous on \(\sigma(T_1)\cup\sigma(T_2)\),
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonal_cfc` proves
\[
  f(\operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2))
    = \operatorname{diag}(f(T_1),f(T_2)).
\]
The proof uses the continuous star-algebra homomorphism
`cstarMatrixBlockDiagonalStarAlgHom_continuous` and mathlib's product CFC
map theorem.  The remaining source theorem is not this block-diagonal CFC
step; it is the compression/Jensen inequality for the block isometry.

A1.5-B1 range-projection/reflection update: A1.5ck through A1.5co close the next block-isometry
dependency for the same source proof.  For \(V=[A;B]\), the range projection
\(P=VV^*\) is formalized and proved self-adjoint and idempotent under
\(V^*V=I\); the absorption identities \(PV=V\) and \(V^*P=V^*\) are also
proved.  The associated reflection \(R=2P-I\) is now proved self-adjoint,
involutive, a unit, unitary, and range-fixing.  The unitary-conjugation CFC
identity \(f(UTU^*)=Uf(T)U^*\) is also closed, and unitary/range-reflection
conjugation is proved to preserve strict positivity.  The compressed block
argument also preserves strict positivity:
\(V^*\operatorname{diag}(T_1,T_2)V=A^*T_1A+B^*T_2B\) is strictly positive when
\(T_1,T_2\) are.  This moves the active theorem from reflection algebra, CFC
conjugation, and domain preservation to the actual nonlinear
pinching/compression Jensen inequality or a finite Effros perspective theorem.

A1.5-B1 Bendat--Sherman derivative-monotonicity update: A1.5bm proves
`cstarMatrixXLogXDerivativeMonotoneTarget_of_log_monotone`, after the CFC
normalization `cstarMatrix_cfc_one_add_log_eq_one_add_log`.  This closes the
operator-monotonicity of \(1+\log x\), the scalar derivative of \(x\log x\),
on the strictly positive C-star-matrix cone by reusing the local
`cstarMatrix_log_le_log` theorem. It does not prove the Bendat--Sherman bridge
from derivative/divided-difference monotonicity to operator convexity.

A1.5-B1 Bendat--Sherman bridge target-lock update: A1.5bn names the
derivative-only diagnostic bridge as
`cstarMatrixBendatShermanDerivativeBridgeTarget` and proves the adapter
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_of_bendatShermanDerivativeBridge`.
This removed an ambiguity in an earlier route formulation, but A1.5bo below
supersedes it as the source-faithful Bendat--Sherman route statement.

A1.5-B1 Bendat--Sherman route correction: A1.5bo records the more
source-faithful divided-difference route.  The route now names
`realXLogXDividedDifference`,
`cstarMatrixXLogXDividedDifferenceMonotoneTarget`, and
`cstarMatrixBendatShermanDividedDifferenceBridgeTarget`, plus the adapter
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_of_bendatShermanDividedDifferenceBridge`.
This prevents the derivative-monotonicity theorem A1.5bm from being mistaken
for the full Bendat--Sherman source theorem.

A1.5-B1 divided-difference scalar-normalization update: A1.5bp proves the
off-diagonal scalar identities
`realXLogXDividedDifference_eq_log_add_ratio` and
`realXLogXDividedDifference_eq_log_add_normalized`, plus the diagonal
normalization `realXLogXDividedDifference_self`.  This identifies the scalar
kernel that a future integral-representation proof must show is
operator-monotone.  It does not close the operator-monotonicity target or the
Bendat--Sherman bridge.

A1.5-B1 fractional-kernel update: A1.5bq proves
`cstarMatrix_spectrum_nonneg_of_nonneg`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_one_sub_one_add_inv_monotone`, and
`cstarMatrix_cfc_pos_over_one_add_monotone`.  This closes the first unital CFC
operator-monotonicity dependency for the normalized logarithmic-kernel
integral route.  It does not prove the scaled kernel, the integral lift, full
divided-difference monotonicity, or the Bendat--Sherman bridge.

A1.5-B1 scaled fractional-kernel update: A1.5br proves
`cstarMatrix_cfc_pos_over_pos_add_monotone`, the operator-monotonicity theorem
for \(x \mapsto x/(s+x)\) on the nonnegative C-star cone for every \(s>0\).
This closes the scaled fractional-kernel dependency by scaling to the already
proved \(x/(1+x)\) case.  It does not prove the integral lift to the
normalized logarithmic kernel, full divided-difference monotonicity, or the
Bendat--Sherman bridge.

A1.5-B1 finite-combination update: A1.5bs proves
`cstarMatrix_cfc_finset_sum_nonneg_mul_pos_over_pos_add_monotone`, showing
that finite nonnegative linear combinations of the scaled kernels
\(x\mapsto x/(\sigma+x)\), \(\sigma>0\), are operator-monotone on the
nonnegative C-star cone.  This is the finite-sum precursor to the
integral-representation route.  It does not prove the Bochner/Riemann integral
lift, full divided-difference monotonicity, or the Bendat--Sherman bridge.

A1.5-B1 CFC integral-order update: A1.5bt proves
`cfc_integral_mono_of_forall_of_bound`, the generic theorem that pointwise CFC
Loewner inequalities integrate to Loewner inequalities for the integrated
kernel under the joint-continuity and finite-integral-bound hypotheses used by
mathlib's `cfc_integral`.  This closes the order-theoretic Bochner-integral
substrate, but it still does not prove the scalar-integral-to-CFC equality,
full divided-difference monotonicity, or the Bendat--Sherman bridge.  The
concrete continuity and boundedness side-condition layer is closed later by
A1.5bw.

A1.5-B1 scalar logarithmic-integral update: A1.5bu defines
`realNormalizedLogKernel`, proves
`realXLogXDividedDifference_eq_log_add_normalizedKernel`, and proves
`real_normalizedLogKernel_offdiag_intervalIntegral`, the off-diagonal scalar
identity
\[
  \int_0^1 \frac{t}{u+(1-u)t}\,du=\frac{t\log t}{t-1}
\]
for \(t>0\), \(t\ne1\).  This closes the scalar identity side of the integral
route; A1.5bw separately closes continuity and boundedness side conditions.
The next dependency after this scalar identity was the scalar-integral-to-CFC
equality; that equality and the normalized-kernel CFC monotonicity theorem are
closed later by A1.5bx.

A1.5-B1 unit-interval integrand update: A1.5bv proves
`cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_monotone`, the pointwise CFC
monotonicity theorem for
\[
  x\mapsto \frac{x}{u+(1-u)x},\qquad 0<u<1.
\]
The proof reuses A1.5br with \(s=u/(1-u)\).  A1.5bv also proves
`cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_monotone_of_mem_Icc`, which
handles all \(u\in[0,1]\) on the strictly positive cone by treating \(u=0\) as
the constant-one CFC kernel and \(u=1\) as the identity kernel.  This closes the
pointwise integrand side of the scalar integral route; the remaining concrete
`cfc_integral` side conditions and scalar-integral-to-CFC equality are still
open before `cstarMatrixXLogXDividedDifferenceMonotoneTarget` can close.

A1.5-B1 `cfc_integral` side-condition update: A1.5bw proves
`continuousOn_uncurry_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_spectrum`,
`real_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_abs_le_max_of_le`,
`real_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max`,
`ae_unit_interval_fractional_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max`,
`hasFiniteIntegral_const_max_one_spectrum_bound`,
`continuousOn_uncurry_unit_interval_subtype_fractional_kernel_spectrum`,
`ae_unit_interval_subtype_fractional_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max`, and
`hasFiniteIntegral_unit_interval_subtype_const_max_one_spectrum_bound`.  This
closes the explicit continuity and boundedness side-condition layer for the
unit-interval fractional-kernel family, including the subtype-domain shape
expected by the future `cfc_integral` assembly.  The scalar-integral-to-CFC
equality for `realNormalizedLogKernel` is closed later by A1.5bx; the full
`cstarMatrixXLogXDividedDifferenceMonotoneTarget` is closed later by A1.5by.

A1.5-B1 normalized logarithmic-kernel CFC update: A1.5bx proves
`realNormalizedLogKernel_setIntegral`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_realNormalizedLogKernel_eq_unit_interval_integral`,
`cstarMatrix_setIntegral_mono_on`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_realNormalizedLogKernel_monotone_of_spectrum_bound`, and
`cstarMatrix_cfc_realNormalizedLogKernel_monotone`.  This closes the
scalar-integral-to-CFC equality and the operator monotonicity theorem for the
normalized logarithmic kernel on the strictly positive finite C-star cone.  The
remaining divided-difference dependency at this point is the base-point scaling
and constant-shift CFC normalization that identifies
`cfc (realXLogXDividedDifference c) A` with
`log c • I + cfc realNormalizedLogKernel (c⁻¹ • A)` on positive spectra,
closed next by A1.5by.

A1.5-B1 divided-difference monotonicity update: A1.5by proves
`realNormalizedLogKernel_eq_mul_dslope_log`,
`continuousOn_realNormalizedLogKernel_Ioi`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_realXLogXDividedDifference_eq_log_add_scaled_normalizedKernel`,
and
`cstarMatrixXLogXDividedDifferenceMonotoneTarget_of_normalizedLogKernel`.
This closes the source-faithful monotonicity target
`cstarMatrixXLogXDividedDifferenceMonotoneTarget` by reducing each base point
`c > 0` to a constant shift plus the already proved normalized logarithmic
kernel applied to `c⁻¹ • A`.  The remaining Bendat--Sherman-route dependency is
now the finite divided-difference bridge
`cstarMatrixBendatShermanDividedDifferenceBridgeTarget`, which should turn the
closed divided-difference monotonicity theorem into
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget`.

A1.5-B1 local-reuse route-elimination update: a repository/mathlib search found
no existing operator Jensen theorem, matrix relative-entropy joint-convexity
theorem, arbitrary-\(H\) Lieb trace-concavity theorem, or matrix Bernstein
theorem to reuse.  In particular, mathlib's CFC exponential/log order file
proves operator-log monotonicity but records operator-log concavity and
operator convexity of \(x\mapsto x\log x\) as TODOs.  The repository has since
closed `cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget` locally, so the remaining
local-reuse negative result is now about
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenTransferAllFiniteTarget` and the downstream
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget`.  These are not hidden in the
dependency graph; they must be proved locally from the Hansen--Pedersen/Effros
source route or the route must change deliberately.

A1.5-B1 covering-route update: A1.5ai closes the product-grid cover
bookkeeping dependency through `abs_coord_le_vecNorm2`,
`realUnitIntervalCover`, `rectUnitBallCover_product_grid`, and
`fintype_card_product_grid_index`. This does not change the primary
trace-MGF bottleneck, and it does not prove equation (2); it only means the
alternative covering route no longer has to assume the `n`-dimensional product
cover/cardinality step once a one-dimensional interval grid is supplied.

## Current Gate Result

The full-paper gate is **FAIL** because A1.5, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine,
the still-open solver/preconditioner and random-projection sides of LS.3,
A3.4, LR.1, MC.1, and LAP.1 remain open paper-level items. Closed deterministic
subtheorems, conditional transfers, and the now-closed Algorithm 2 leverage
row-sampled LS sample-budget theorem remain valuable library results, but they
do not close the open randomized-projection, structural, optimization,
implementation-backed solver, or graph-sparsification claims.

Under the automation prompt, this FAIL status is a continuation checkpoint, not
a stopping condition.  The newest closed frontier is A1.5cm: after A1.5cf
proved ordinary positive-cone operator convexity of \(x\log x\), A1.5ch packages
that proof at every finite matrix size and A1.5ci closes the block-column,
block-diagonal star-algebra, and block-order substrate used by the standard
Hansen--Pedersen proof.  A1.5cj additionally closes the block-diagonal CFC
decomposition, A1.5ck closes the range-projection algebra for \(P=VV^*\),
A1.5cl closes the reflection algebra for \(R=2P-I\), and A1.5cm closes CFC
under unitary conjugation.  The next active theorem is therefore the nonlinear
pinching/reflection average or compression/Jensen part of the functional-calculus transfer theorem
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenTransferAllFiniteTarget` or a finite Effros
perspective theorem that bypasses it. A1.5by previously
proved the full source-faithful divided-difference monotonicity target for
\(x\log x\) on strictly positive finite C-star matrices by reducing it to the
normalized logarithmic kernel.  A1.5bx previously closed the
scalar-integral-to-CFC equality and normalized-kernel operator monotonicity.
A1.5bo previously named the source-faithful Bendat--Sherman divided-difference
route and proved the adapter showing that the divided-difference bridge plus
divided-difference monotonicity would close the concrete \(x\log x\)
operator-convexity input. A1.5bn previously named the derivative-only bridge
target and adapter.
A1.5bm previously proved the Bendat--Sherman-route derivative-monotonicity
subdependency for \(1+\log x\) using local operator-log monotonicity. A1.5bl previously proved
the source-route assembly adapter from the concrete \(x\log x\)
operator-convexity target plus the concrete Hansen--Pedersen transfer target
to the assembled two-point Jensen target. A1.5bk previously separated the
Effros/Hansen--Pedersen route into ordinary positive-cone operator convexity,
Hansen--Pedersen Jensen transfer, and the assembled two-point Jensen target,
proving only the identity-function sanity case for the ordinary convexity
shape. The latest route-elimination check also rules out a simple local/mathlib
reuse path for the missing nonlinear operator-convexity/Jensen theorem. A1.5bi
previously closed the finite
Kronecker trace substrate
\(\operatorname{tr}(A\otimes H)=\operatorname{tr}(A)\operatorname{tr}(H)\)
and the identity-lift trace normalizations for the Tropp/Effros
operator-perspective route. A1.5bh previously closed the finite Kronecker
lift substrate \(A\mapsto A\otimes I\) and \(H\mapsto I\otimes H\), including
affine laws, commutation, multiplication to \(A\otimes H\), and
positive-definiteness preservation. A1.5bg previously closed the product and
natural-power algebra for \(L_A\) and \(R_A\), following the explicit
\(L_X R_A^{-1}\) ratio endomorphism and base-point normalization from
A1.5bf. A1.5be previously closed the left/right
multiplication endomorphism substrate. A1.5bd previously closed the finite log-sum
inequality, scalar/vector relative-entropy joint convexity, and the
real-diagonal C-star matrix joint-convexity subcase. A1.5bc
previously closed the compact Hermitian generalized-Klein bridge, local
generalized Klein, local matrix relative-entropy nonnegativity, the normalized
entropy variational formula, and the reduction from relative-entropy joint
convexity alone to the local Lieb target. These are deterministic
matrix-analysis foundations for the Tropp/Lieb route only. They do not prove
noncommutative matrix relative-entropy joint convexity, arbitrary
self-adjoint-`H` Lieb trace concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, or equation (2). A1.5al previously
added local `exp(log A)=A` wrappers for strictly positive finite complex C-star
matrices, in normed-space, complex CFC, and real CFC exponential forms. A1.5ai
previously added a product-grid
cover theorem showing that a one-dimensional interval grid for `[-1,1]`
induces an `n`-dimensional Euclidean unit-ball cover with radius loss
`sqrt n`, and the product index type has cardinality `|grid|^n`.
This is a covering-route dependency only.  It does not prove the finite-grid
scalar tail rates, trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, or
equation (2). A1.5af previously added a complex C⋆ trace bridge showing that
`cstarMatrixTrace` agrees with `finiteTrace` on embedded finite real matrices,
transfers finite PSD/Loewner trace nonnegativity/monotonicity to real parts,
proves cyclicity `tr(MN)=tr(NM)`, proves real-part positivity/monotonicity for
the C⋆ spectral order, and proves scalar CFC-exponential trace normalization
for complex and real scalar identities.
A1.5ae previously added a
finite-real-to-complex-C⋆ regularization/log bridge showing that embedded local
PSD matrices become strictly positive after adding a positive scalar identity,
regularized finite Loewner inequalities stay ordered, and local finite Loewner
inequalities therefore imply regularized operator-log inequalities.  These are
trace/functional-calculus adapters, not the CACM equation
(2) trace-MGF domination theorem.  A1.5ab previously added a weak accumulated
bounded-increment theorem showing that the probability-one truncated two-sided
Loewner bounded-increment event implies a probability-one exact truncated
spectral event at the worst-case accumulated scale `sL`.  This is an audit
theorem for the bounded-increment layer, not the CACM equation (2) rate.
A1.5aa previously added a scalar Loewner-to-trace-exponential bound
`M <= c I -> tr(exp(M)) <= d exp(c)` and the negative companion
`-M <= c I -> tr(exp(-M)) <= d exp(c)`.  It also has upper-, lower-, and
two-sided finite-probability trace-exponential/eigenvalue interfaces.  A1.5x
bounds the probability that some Hermitian eigenvalue is at
least a threshold by `exp(-T)` times the expected trace exponential, with
scalar-bound and high-probability complement forms.  A1.5y supplies the lower
tail using `tr(exp(-M))`, bounding the probability that some eigenvalue is at
most a threshold by `exp(T)` times the expected negative trace exponential,
again with scalar-bound and high-probability complement forms.  A1.5z combines
the two sides into an all-absolute-eigenvalues-below-threshold event. A1.5aa
uses the Hermitian eigenvector basis to show that a scalar-identity Loewner CGF
bound can be converted into a scalar trace-exponential bound.  A1.5w previously proved the
repository-native power-series matrix-exponential trace identity
`tr(exp(M)) = sum_i exp(lambda_i(M))` for local finite real symmetric matrices,
via Hermitian spectral diagonalization, matrix-exponential conjugation, trace
cyclicity, and the diagonal exponential formula.  A1.5v previously added the
Hermitian continuous-functional-calculus exponential interface and proved
`tr(E_cfc(M)) = sum_i exp(lambda_i(M))`; A1.5u previously added the mathlib-backed
finite matrix exponential, proved the scalar-identity trace normalization
`tr(exp(L I)) = d exp(L)`, preserved symmetry through matrix exponentiation, and
proved the finite diagonal normalization `tr(exp(diag v)) = sum_i exp(v_i)`.
These are prerequisites for a future trace-MGF argument.
A1.5t previously exposed an eigenvalue
event for the dilation, added a finite union-bound adapter from supplied
single-eigenvalue probability bounds, and transferred these events to exact/FP
rectangular spectral events.
A1.5s proves that local PSD and scalar-identity Loewner upper bounds are
equivalent to nonnegativity of the Hermitian eigenvalues of the relevant
difference matrix, with named scalar upper-difference eigenvalues.
A1.5r gives local
symmetric finite real matrices repository-named Hermitian eigenvalues routed
through mathlib, with spectrum membership and trace-equals-sum-of-eigenvalues.
A1.5p/A1.5q also bridge the repository's finite symmetry, PSD, and Loewner
predicates to mathlib's `Matrix.IsSymm`, `Matrix.IsHermitian`, and
`Matrix.PosSemidef` APIs. A1.5o closed the one-sided
self-adjoint dilation Loewner event transfer from `D(R) <= eps I` to exact and
floating-point rectangular spectral events. A1.5n also closed the product trace finite-family scalar MGF
support, pointwise-bound-to-MGF adapters, support-aware pointwise variants, and
self-adjoint-dilation quadratic-form specializations for supplied finite test
vectors. These complement A1.5k's product-law zero-mean and expected-variance
bridges. Together with A1.5j, this leaves the Drineas--Zouzias truncated route
with product-law support, bounded-increment, two-sided Loewner boundedness,
bounded-square, scalar-MGF,
finite-test quadratic-form MGF, support-aware pointwise-MGF bookkeeping,
one-sided dilation-event transfer, eigenvalue-event transfer, mathlib
PSD/Loewner/spectral bridges, matrix-exponential scalar/diagonal normalization,
Hermitian CFC trace diagonalization, repository-native matrix-exponential trace
diagonalization, MGF-to-eigenvalue tail/complement interface, regularized
C⋆/operator-log bridge, and variance prerequisites. The next proof target is no
longer the deterministic dilation bridge, one-sided/eigenvalue or
squared-Loewner adapter, variance proxy, product-law expectation bridge, scalar
product-law MGF bridge, finite-family MGF adapter, pointwise/support-aware MGF
adapter, truncation transfer layer, support lemma, bounded-increment side
condition, scalar/diagonal matrix-exponential normalization, Hermitian CFC
trace diagonalization, power-series matrix-exponential trace diagonalization,
two-sided Loewner boundedness package, MGF-to-eigenvalue tail/complement
interface, regularized-log bridge, C⋆ trace bridge, or self-adjoint
`log(exp X)=X` bridge; it is the first genuine spectral concentration
foundation:

- trace-exponential domination / trace-MGF control for sums of independent
  self-adjoint increments, plus its Bernstein/Khintchine instantiation; or
- a covering route with explicit finite covers and sharper fixed-vector tails.

The source-alignment transfer layer for the Drineas--Zouzias truncation has
now been formalized, but A1.5 still requires the actual matrix-Bernstein
half-budget theorem for sampling `\hat A`; the untruncated `A_ij^2 / ||A||_F^2`
law remains a separate theorem target unless explicitly narrowed.

The first route is closer to the CACM equation (2) statement and would also
unlock LS.3, but it requires substantially heavier spectral foundations.

### A1.5-B1 Frontier Theorem

The next theorem is not another deterministic adapter.  A local repository and
mathlib search found no existing Golden-Thompson, Lieb trace-concavity,
trace-MGF domination, matrix Bernstein, or matrix Khintchine theorem.  The
latest bottleneck-locality pass also checked mathlib's continuous functional
calculus files: `ExpLog.Order` provides operator-log monotonicity but lists
operator-log concavity as a TODO, while `Rpow.Order` lists operator concavity of
`rpow` as a TODO.  Thus the needed concavity input is not a hidden local theorem
waiting to be wrapped.
The active blocker and its dependencies are also recorded in
`docs/RANDNLA_CACM_BOTTLENECK_LEDGER.md`.
The
blocking foundation is the finite-dimensional Lieb/Tropp trace step, in
mathematical form

\[
  \mathbb E\,\operatorname{tr}\exp(H+X)
  \le
  \operatorname{tr}\exp\!\left(H+\log(\mathbb E\,\exp X)\right),
\]

for deterministic self-adjoint \(H\) and a random self-adjoint matrix \(X\)
with strictly positive regularized \(\mathbb E\exp X\), followed by the
standard iteration over independent summands.  In Lean this will require a
local theorem family for the trace exponential of C\(^*\)-matrix sums.  The
finite-probability expectation/order vocabulary for complex scalars and complex
\(\texttt{CStarMatrix}\)-valued random variables is now closed in A1.5ag,
including expectation positivity, monotonicity, and strict-positive
regularization of nonnegative expectations.  The finite Jensen adapter for
concave real-valued functions of C\(^*\)-matrix random variables is now closed
in A1.5ah.  The theorem must not assume Lieb trace concavity or the trace-MGF
bound as a hypothesis; that would only recreate the already-proved last-mile
Markov interface.

Chosen route:

- continue with the Lieb concavity route from Tropp's Theorem 3.6 and
  Corollary 3.7, reusing the existing operator-log, strict-positive
  regularization, trace positivity, trace cyclicity, scalar CFC-exponential
  trace bridge, the C⋆ expectation/order bridge, and the finite Jensen adapter.

Rejected or deferred alternatives:

- formalize an Ahlswede-Winter/Golden-Thompson route, which is older and may
  have worse constants or additional symmetrization obligations;
- abandon trace-MGF for a covering-net route, which avoids Lieb but requires an
  explicit finite-net construction, cardinality bounds, and sharper
  fixed-vector tails.

With the relative-entropy route chosen, the next dependency is a relative-entropy
foundation: `cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`.  The
variational-principle and nonnegativity sides are now closed by A1.5bc, and
`liebTraceConcavityTarget_of_relativeEntropy_jointConvex` will turn joint
convexity into the finite-dimensional Lieb trace-concavity target. Once that
closes, the existing finite Jensen adapter can be used to prove the
nonconditional one-step Tropp trace-MGF theorem.

Latest A1.5-B1 route-split update: A1.5bz closes the finite matrix
arithmetic-harmonic mean / inverse-convexity theorem
`matrix_inv_convex_posDef` by a Schur-complement argument, then lifts it back
to finite C-star order and CFC form through
`cstarMatrix_cfc_inv_convex_isStrictlyPositive`.  This is a genuine
source-standard dependency for a direct shifted-inverse integral representation
of operator convexity of \(x\log x\).  A follow-up closes the shifted-positive
kernel family as `cstarMatrix_cfc_shifted_inv_convex_nonneg`; the direct route
next required the \(x\log x\) integral representation theorem.

Latest A1.5-B1 direct-route closure: the source-aligned kernel has been
corrected to
\[
  (u,x)\mapsto \frac{x(x-1)}{u+(1-u)x},
\]
whose integral over \([0,1]\) is \(x\log x\).  The earlier
\((x-1)^2/(u+(1-u)x)\) decomposition remains true and useful as an auxiliary,
but it is not the reconstruction kernel for \(x\log x\).  The new Lean
theorems `real_xlog_eq_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integral`,
`real_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_eq_affine_add_shifted_inv`,
`continuousOn_uncurry_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum`,
`real_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max_sq`,
`ae_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_spectrum_norm_le_max_sq`,
`hasFiniteIntegral_const_max_one_spectrum_bound_sq`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_xlog_eq_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integral`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_unit_interval_xlog_kernel_integrand_convex_of_pos_lt_one`,
and `cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`
close the ordinary positive-cone operator-convexity target
`cstarMatrixXLogXPositiveOperatorConvexTarget`.
