Latest A1.5-B1 Hansen--Pedersen closure: the nonlinear \(x\log x\) corner
functional-calculus identity is now proved.  The new infrastructure
`cstarMatrix_complex_finiteDimensional`, the compression linearity/CLM lemmas
`cstarMatrix_compression_add`, `cstarMatrix_compression_sub`,
`cstarMatrix_compression_smul`, `cstarMatrix_compression_real_smul`,
`cstarMatrixCompressionCLM`, and `cstarMatrix_compression_setIntegral`, plus
strict-positive compression
`cstarMatrix_compression_isStrictlyPositive_of_injective_mulVec` and
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_compression_isStrictlyPositive_of_sum`, assemble the
unit-interval kernel corner
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_reflectionAverage_xlog_kernel_corner_of_sum` into
`cstarMatrixColumnPair_reflectionAverage_xlog_corner_of_sum`.  Combining that
corner with the already proved reflection-average CFC pinching inequality gives
the concrete two-point Hansen--Pedersen Jensen theorem for \(x\log x\):
`cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_reflectionAverage_xlog_corner`,
packaged as `cstarMatrixXLogXHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`.
The next affine-correction dependency for the relative-entropy perspective is
also now closed: `realEntropyKernel` names \(x\log x-(x-1)\),
`cstarMatrix_cfc_realEntropyKernel_eq_xlog_sub_id_add_one` expands its CFC on
strictly positive matrices,
`cstarMatrixEntropyKernelPositiveOperatorConvexTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`
and
`cstarMatrixEntropyKernelPositiveOperatorConvexAllFiniteTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`
prove ordinary finite-size operator convexity for this normalized kernel, and
`cstarMatrixEntropyKernelHansenPedersenJensenTarget_of_unit_interval_kernel`
proves the two-point Hansen--Pedersen Jensen theorem for that normalized
entropy kernel.

Latest A1.5-B1 perspective substrate closure: the finite CFC square-root layer
is now formalized.  The new names `cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt`, `cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_isSelfAdjoint`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_isSelfAdjoint`,
`continuousOn_real_inv_sqrt_spectrum_of_isStrictlyPositive`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_mul_self`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_mul_sqrt`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_mul_invSqrt`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_isUnit`,
`complex_ofReal_sqrt_mul_self_of_nonneg`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_isStrictlyPositive`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_mul_self_mul`, and
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_conj_isStrictlyPositive` close the local
\(A^{1/2}\)/\(A^{-1/2}\) identities and the strict-positivity congruence needed
before a perspective theorem can be stated without hidden square-root
hypotheses.

Latest A1.5-B1 ordinary perspective closure: the finite perspective theorem for
the normalized entropy kernel is now formalized.  The new names
`cstarMatrixPerspective`, `cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeight`,
`cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeight_star_mul_self`,
`cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeights_star_mul_self_add`,
`cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeight_compress_normalized`,
`cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeight_mul_positiveSqrt`,
`cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_mul_perspectiveWeight_star`,
`cstarMatrixPerspectiveWeight_value_uncompress`, and
`cstarMatrixEntropyKernelPerspective_jointConvex` prove joint convexity of
\(P_f(X,A)=A^{1/2}f(A^{-1/2}XA^{-1/2})A^{1/2}\) for
\(f(x)=x\log x-(x-1)\).  This closes the ordinary finite perspective
dependency, but it is not the source-faithful superoperator trace
representation for Umegaki relative entropy.

Latest A1.5-B1 superoperator trace-substrate closure: finite vectorization and
the vectorized-identity trace pairing are now formalized.  The new names
`matrixVecId`, `matrixVec`, `matrixComplexQuadraticForm`,
`finset_sum_product_diagonal`,
`matrix_kronecker_transpose_mulVec_matrixVec`, and
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose` prove that
\(A\otimes B^{\mathsf T}\) represents \(M\mapsto AMB\) and that
\(v_I^*(A\otimes B^{\mathsf T})v_I=\operatorname{tr}(AB)\).  These are direct
dependencies for the remaining finite superoperator perspective trace
representation, but not yet the full relative-entropy trace formula.

Latest A1.5-B1 polynomial trace-substrate closure: powers of the Kronecker
left-right lift now have the source-faithful action and trace-pairing formulas.
The new names `matrix_kronecker_transpose_pow`,
`matrix_kronecker_transpose_pow_mulVec_matrixVec`, `matrixVec_one`, and
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_pow` prove
\((A\otimes B^{\mathsf T})^k=A^k\otimes(B^k)^{\mathsf T}\),
\((A\otimes B^{\mathsf T})^k\operatorname{vec}(M)=\operatorname{vec}(A^kMB^k)\),
and \(v_I^*(A\otimes B^{\mathsf T})^k v_I=\operatorname{tr}(A^kB^k)\).  This
closes the polynomial trace-representation layer but not the CFC/log transfer
or the Umegaki relative-entropy trace formula.

Latest A1.5-B1 finite-polynomial trace-substrate closure: the powered trace
identity is now packaged for arbitrary finite polynomial combinations.  The
new names `matrixComplexQuadraticForm_sum`, `matrixComplexQuadraticForm_smul`,
and `matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_polynomial` prove
\[
  v_I^*\left(\sum_{k\in S} c_k(A\otimes B^{\mathsf T})^k\right)v_I
  =
  \sum_{k\in S}c_k\operatorname{tr}(A^kB^k).
\]
This is still finite algebraic substrate; the open step is the analytic
CFC/log transfer and the source-faithful relative-entropy trace formula.

Latest A1.5-B1 standard-polynomial interface closure: the same trace identity
is now stated in Lean's `Polynomial.aeval` vocabulary as
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_aeval`.  This is a
small but necessary API bridge for the next functional-calculus pass; it does
not close the analytic CFC/log theorem.

Latest A1.5-B1 analytic-transfer substrate closure: the finite quadratic form
used in the vectorized trace pairing is now proved continuous in the matrix
argument by `continuous_matrixComplexQuadraticForm`.  This supplies the first
limit-passage hook for a future polynomial-to-CFC/log theorem, but it does not
prove that theorem.

Latest A1.5-B1 polynomial-continuity closure: `continuous_matrix_polynomial_aeval`
proves continuity of \(M\mapsto p(M)\) for each complex polynomial \(p\) on
finite complex matrices.  This clears another finite-dimensional continuity
side condition for the next approximation/CFC step.

Latest A1.5-B1 superoperator-polynomial perspective closure: the finite
right-multiplication trace representation for the source-faithful
Effros/Umegaki route is now formalized.  The new domain/CFC/approximation
theorems are `matrixVecId_inner_matrixVec`,
`matrix_transpose_conjTranspose_eq_self_of_isSelfAdjoint`,
`matrix_kronecker_transpose_isSelfAdjoint_of_isSelfAdjoint`,
`matrix_kronecker_transpose_posSemidef`, `matrix_kronecker_transpose_posDef`,
`matrix_kronecker_inv_transpose_posDef`, `matrixSelfAdjointCfc`,
`matrixSelfAdjointCfc_polynomial`, `exists_realPolynomial_near_log_on_Icc`,
`exists_realPolynomial_near_xlog_on_Icc`, and
`exists_realPolynomial_near_realEntropyKernel_on_Icc`.  The new trace formulas
are `matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_pow_right`,
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_polynomial_right`,
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_kronecker_transpose_aeval_right`,
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_matrixSelfAdjointCfc_kronecker_transpose_realPolynomial`,
and
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_vecId_matrixSelfAdjointCfc_kronecker_inv_transpose_realPolynomial_right`.
This closes the finite-polynomial identity for
\(v_I^*p(L_XR_A^{-1})R_Av_I\).  It still leaves open the analytic
logarithmic/entropy-kernel CFC transfer and the Umegaki relative-entropy trace
representation, so the full-paper gate remains FAIL.

Latest A1.5-B1 analytic CFC transfer closure:
`tendsto_matrixComplexQuadraticForm_matrixSelfAdjointCfc_mul` proves that
uniform convergence of scalar functions on the real spectrum of a finite
self-adjoint matrix transfers through matrix CFC, right multiplication, and
finite quadratic-form trace pairing.  The specialized theorem
`tendsto_superoperator_entropyKernel_of_realPolynomial_uniform_approx` applies
this to \(K=X\otimes(A^{-1})^{\mathsf T}\) and \(R=I\otimes A^{\mathsf T}\):
any explicit uniform real-polynomial approximation to
\(x\log x-(x-1)\) sends the finite-polynomial trace formulas to the
normalized entropy-kernel CFC trace term.  This closes the listed analytic
transfer dependency, but it still leaves open the construction of the full
source-faithful Umegaki trace representation and noncommutative
relative-entropy joint convexity, so the full-paper gate remains FAIL.

Latest A1.5-B1 concrete approximation closure:
`matrix_posDef_spectrum_real_pos` and
`matrix_posDef_spectrum_real_subset_Icc` prove that a positive definite finite
complex matrix has real spectrum contained in a positive compact interval.
`exists_realPolynomial_tendstoUniformlyOn_realEntropyKernel_on_subset_Icc` and
`exists_realPolynomial_tendstoUniformlyOn_realEntropyKernel_spectrum_of_posDef`
construct a real-polynomial sequence converging uniformly to
\(x\log x-(x-1)\) on such spectra.  The wrapper
`exists_realPolynomial_tendsto_superoperator_entropyKernel_trace_of_posDef`
combines this construction with the analytic CFC transfer for
\(K=X\otimes(A^{-1})^{\mathsf T}\).  Thus the earlier supplied-approximation
input is no longer a bottleneck hypothesis.  The active blocker is now the
source-faithful Umegaki trace representation from the resulting CFC trace term,
then noncommutative relative-entropy joint convexity.

Latest A1.5-B1 trace-representation correction:
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator` now names the
remaining source-faithful trace representation exactly, using the relative
modular operator \(L_XR_A^{-1}\) via \(X\otimes(A^{-1})^{\mathsf T}\) and right
multiplication by \(A\).  The ordinary source-matrix perspective bridge is not
used, because it is not the Umegaki superoperator representation required by
the Tropp--Effros route.  The representation itself remains open.

Latest A1.5-B1 compact-relative-entropy overlap closure:
`matrix_isHermitian_cfc_id`, `matrix_isHermitian_cfc_xlog`, and
`matrixTrace_hermitianCfc_relativeEntropy_re_eq_sum` identify the compact
matrix trace expression
\(\operatorname{Re}\operatorname{tr}(X\log X-X\log A-(X-A))\) with the
finite scalar relative-entropy overlap sum
\(\sum_{i,j} d(\lambda_i,\mu_j)|\langle u_i,v_j\rangle|^2\).  This closes the
repository-\(D(X;A)\) side of the Umegaki representation route.  The remaining
active blocker is the other half: proving that the source-faithful
superoperator CFC term for \(L_XR_A^{-1}\) has the same overlap expansion, and
then using it to prove noncommutative joint convexity.

Latest A1.5-B1 superoperator-overlap adapter:
`matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelTrace` now packages the finite Kronecker
superoperator expression, `matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion`
names the exact remaining spectral-overlap theorem for that expression, and
`matrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentation_of_superoperator_overlap` proves that
this overlap theorem would identify the superoperator trace with the compact
relative-entropy trace.  This is only a bottleneck adapter; the overlap theorem
itself remains open.

Latest A1.5-B1 finite-polynomial overlap closure:
`matrix_isHermitian_cfc_congr_eigen`, `matrix_isHermitian_cfc_mul`,
`matrix_isHermitian_cfc_fun_pow_nat`,
`matrix_isHermitian_cfc_inv_of_posDef`, and
`matrix_posDef_mul_inv_pow_eq_cfc` supply the fixed-Hermitian CFC algebra for
powers and inverses.  Using those facts,
`matrixTrace_pow_mul_inv_pow_re_eq_sum` proves the overlap expansion for
\(\operatorname{Re}\operatorname{tr}(X^kA(A^{-1})^k)\), and
`matrixPolynomialTraceRatio_re_eq_sum` sums it to the real-polynomial form
\(\sum_{i,j}\mu_jp(\lambda_i/\mu_j)|(U_X^*U_A)_{ij}|^2\).  This closes the
finite-polynomial overlap dependency of
`matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion`, but the limiting
entropy-kernel CFC overlap expansion itself remains open.

The full-paper gate remains **FAIL** because this closes only the concrete
Hansen--Pedersen, perspective-substrate, ordinary finite perspective,
vectorized trace-pairing, finite-polynomial trace-pairing, `Polynomial.aeval`
interface/continuity, concrete uniform approximation, CFC trace-transfer,
compact relative-entropy spectral-overlap, and superoperator-overlap-adapter
dependencies on the path to matrix concentration.  The
active Lean blocker is
now
`matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion`.  Once it closes, the next
target is the transport to
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator`, followed by a
source-faithful proof of
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`; after that come
arbitrary-\(H\) Lieb trace concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, and finally CACM equation (2).

Latest A1.5-B1 limiting-overlap and trace-representation closure:
`realRelativeEntropy_eq_mul_realEntropyKernel_mul_inv` supplies the scalar
identity \(D(a\|b)=b f(a/b)\) for the normalized entropy kernel
\(f(t)=t\log t-(t-1)\).  The analytic limit passage from real-polynomial
overlap formulas to the entropy-kernel overlap formula is now closed by
`tendsto_matrixPolynomialTraceRatio_overlap_sum_of_uniform_approx` and
`exists_realPolynomial_tendsto_superoperator_entropyKernel_trace_and_overlap_of_posDef`.
The finite-dimensional source-faithful overlap theorem itself is now proved in
all cases by `matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion_of_nonempty`,
`matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion_of_isEmpty`, and
`matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelOverlapExpansion_all`.  Finally,
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator_all` transports
this matrix result to the C-star statement
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator`.

Historical note: at this stage the A1.5-B1 red bottleneck had strictly
advanced from the overlap/trace-representation layer to
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`.  Later A1.5-B1
addenda close that chain and the final source-budget exact/FP theorems for the
cited square source-aligned Algorithm 1 route.  The remaining full-paper gate
failure is now caused by other open paper-level rows, not by A1.5-B1.  The
then-remaining chain was noncommutative matrix relative-entropy joint convexity,
arbitrary-\(H\) Lieb trace concavity, trace-MGF domination, matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine, and finally CACM equation (2).

Latest A1.5-B1 joint-convexity route substrate closure: the product-index
superoperator perspective domain has been made explicit.  The new matrix and
C-star lift names are `matrix_kronecker_right_identity_transpose_real_smul_add`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorLeftLift`, `cstarMatrixSuperoperatorRightLift`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorLeftLift_real_smul_add`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorRightLift_real_smul_add`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorLeftLift_isStrictlyPositive`, and
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorRightLift_isStrictlyPositive`.  The scalar extraction
from a product-index Loewner inequality is now supported by
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_re_nonneg_of_posSemidef`,
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_re_mono_of_posSemidef_sub`, and
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_re_mono_of_cstarMatrix_le`.

These theorems do not close A1.5-B1.  They reduce the next target to the
source-faithful finite superoperator perspective step: prove that the
perspective inequality on the product-index lifts gives joint convexity of
\(v_I^* f(L_XR_A^{-1})R_A v_I\), then transport through
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyTraceRepresentationBySuperoperator_all` to
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`.

Latest A1.5-B1 product-index perspective scalar convexity closure: the
ordinary superoperator perspective trace is now named as
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspectiveTrace`, with additivity support
`matrixComplexQuadraticForm_add`.  The theorem
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspectiveTrace_jointConvex` applies the already
formalized ordinary finite perspective theorem
`cstarMatrixEntropyKernelPerspective_jointConvex` to the product-index lifts
`L_X` and `R_A`, then extracts the scalar vectorized-identity quadratic form.

This closes the product-index perspective inequality itself.  The remaining
red bottleneck is now sharpened to the equality bridge
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyPerspectiveTraceRepresentation`, which must identify
the ordinary product-index perspective trace with the local relative entropy.
The conditional theorem
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive_of_perspectiveTraceRepresentation`
proves that this equality bridge is sufficient for
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive`.

Latest A1.5-B1 equality-bridge CFC commutation closure: the normalized
argument inside the ordinary product-index perspective can now be reordered.
Theorems `cstarMatrixSuperoperatorLeftLift_rightLift_commute`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPositiveInvSqrtRightLift_commute_leftLift`, and
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspective_normalizedArgument_reorder` prove that
the left lift \(L_X\) commutes with the right lift \(R_A\), also commutes with
the CFC inverse square root of \(R_A\), and therefore
\[
  R_A^{-1/2}L_XR_A^{-1/2}=L_X(R_A^{-1/2}R_A^{-1/2}).
\]

This is a direct dependency of
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyPerspectiveTraceRepresentation`.  It does not yet
identify \(R_A^{-1/2}R_A^{-1/2}\) with \(R_A^{-1}\), nor does it prove the CFC
identity that turns the ordinary perspective trace into
\(v_I^*f(L_XR_A^{-1})R_Av_I\).

Latest A1.5-B1 inverse-square-root square closure: the generic theorem
`cstarMatrixPositiveInvSqrt_mul_self_eq_unit_inv` proves
\[
  A^{-1/2}A^{-1/2}=A^{-1}
\]
as an equality with the inverse unit supplied by strict positivity.  Combining
this with the product-index reorder gives
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspective_normalizedArgument_eq_leftLift_mul_rightLift_unit_inv`,
which rewrites the normalized perspective argument as \(L_XR_A^{-1}\) in the
right-lift unit representation.

The remaining equality-bridge dependency is now the outer CFC/square-root
commutation step that converts \(R_A^{1/2} f(L_XR_A^{-1})R_A^{1/2}\) into
\(f(L_XR_A^{-1})R_A\) inside the vectorized-identity quadratic form.

Latest A1.5-B1 outer square-root commutation closure: theorems
`cstarMatrixPositiveSqrt_commute_unit_inv`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPositiveSqrtRightLift_commute_leftLift`, and
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorLeftLift_mul_rightLift_unit_inv_commute_positiveSqrtRightLift`
prove that the ratio \(L_XR_A^{-1}\), represented using the right-lift inverse
unit, commutes with the CFC square root \(R_A^{1/2}\).

The remaining equality-bridge work is now the CFC transport itself: prove that
the CFC value \(f(L_XR_A^{-1})\) also commutes with \(R_A^{1/2}\), move the
outer square roots to form \(R_A\), and match the resulting product-index
C-star expression with `matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelTrace`.

Latest A1.5-B1 relative-entropy/Lieb closure: the equality bridge is now
closed.  Theorems
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelCfc_ratio_commute_positiveSqrtRightLift`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspective_outerSqrt_cfc_ratio_mul_outerSqrt`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspective_eq_cfc_ratio_mul_rightLift`,
`cstarMatrix_unit_inv_to_matrix`,
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorRightLift_unit_inv_to_matrix`, and
`cstarMatrixSuperoperatorPerspectiveTrace_eq_matrixSuperoperatorEntropyKernelTrace`
identify the ordinary product-index perspective trace with the previously
formalized source-faithful Umegaki superoperator trace.  The theorem
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyPerspectiveTraceRepresentation_all` then closes the
local relative-entropy perspective trace representation, and
`cstarMatrixRelativeEntropyJointConvexOnStrictPositive_all` closes
finite-dimensional matrix relative-entropy joint convexity on the strictly
positive cone.

Consequently `liebTraceConcavityTarget_all` proves finite-dimensional Lieb
trace concavity for every self-adjoint \(H\), and
`FiniteProbability.expectationReal_trace_normed_exp_add_le` proves the
one-step Tropp trace-MGF inequality without assuming Lieb or relative-entropy
joint convexity.  The full-paper gate is still **FAIL** because the remaining
paper-level Algorithm 1 equation (2) chain still needs the Algorithm 1
product-law trace-MGF iteration, matrix Bernstein/Khintchine, and the
downstream floating-point spectral concentration transfer.  The later
trace-MGF iteration rows below supersede the first of these gaps.

Latest A1.5-B1 product-law trace-MGF adapter closure: added
`LeanFpAnalysis/FP/Algorithms/RandNLA/ElementwiseTraceMGF.lean`.  The new
theorems `sqMagSampleProbability`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationComplex_step_eq`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationCStarMatrix_step_eq`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationCStarMatrix_step_eq_sampleExpectation`, and
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_trace_normed_exp_add_step_le` connect
the no-hidden-Lieb one-step trace-MGF theorem to one coordinate of the
canonical Algorithm 1 squared-magnitude product trace law.  The next theorem
family remains the independent-sum iteration and matrix Bernstein/Khintchine
instantiation for equation (2).

Latest A1.5-B1 iid trace-MGF iteration closure: the independent-sum iteration
is now formalized for the canonical Algorithm 1 product trace law.  Theorems
`sqMagTraceProbMass_snoc` and
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_succ_last_eq` prove the append-last
product-law factorization and conditioning identity.  The shared helper
`cstarMatrix_finset_sum_isSelfAdjoint` lives in
`Analysis/CStarMatrixTrace.lean`.  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_trace_normed_exp_add_sum_le` proves
the iid trace-MGF bound
\[
  \mathbb E \operatorname{Re}\operatorname{tr}\exp(H+\sum_t X_t)
  \le
  \operatorname{Re}\operatorname{tr}\exp(H+\sum_t \log \mathbb E\exp X).
\]
The remaining Algorithm 1 equation (2) theorem family is now the matrix
Bernstein/Khintchine tail conversion and the downstream FP spectral transfer,
not the product-law trace-MGF iteration.

Latest A1.5-B1 finite-real trace-exponential adapter closure: the C-star
trace-MGF theorem has now been connected back to the finite real matrix
trace-exponential expression.  Theorems `finiteComplexCStarMatrix_zero`,
`finiteComplexCStarMatrix_add`, and `finiteComplexCStarMatrix_finset_sum`
show that the real-to-C-star embedding preserves the additive algebra used by
finite sums.  Theorems `finiteComplexCStarMatrixRingHom`,
`finiteComplexCStarMatrixRingHom_continuous`,
`finiteComplexCStarMatrix_finiteMatrixExp`,
`cstarMatrixTrace_normed_exp_finiteComplexCStarMatrix`, and
`cstarMatrixTrace_normed_exp_finiteComplexCStarMatrix_re` show that the
embedding commutes with the finite real matrix exponential and preserves the
real trace.  The algorithm-facing theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_add_sum_le`
therefore proves the finite-real trace-MGF domination statement for Algorithm
1's squared-magnitude trace law.

The active red bottleneck is now strictly narrower: prove the scalar
matrix-CGF/log-MGF bound for the Algorithm 1 dilation increments, then combine
it with the existing trace-exponential/eigenvalue Markov interfaces to obtain
the Bernstein/Khintchine high-probability spectral tail and its floating-point
transfer.

Latest A1.5-B1 residual-dilation trace-MGF instantiation closure: the generic
finite-real trace-MGF bridge has now been specialized to the actual Algorithm
1 self-adjoint dilation residual increments.  The definition
`sqMagTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound` names the logarithmic
trace-MGF upper-bound expression.  The theorem
`rectSelfAdjointDilation_elementwiseSampleResidualIncrement_smul_symmetric`
discharges the symmetry side condition for \(\theta D(Z_t)\).
The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_sum_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_le`
instantiates the trace-MGF theorem with the one-sample dilation increments,
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_expectationReal_finiteTrace_finiteMatrixExp_rectSelfAdjointDilation_elementwiseTraceResidual_le`
rewrites the left side as
\(\mathbb E\,\operatorname{tr}\exp(\theta D(A-\widetilde A))\).

Latest A1.5-B1 trace-exponential eigenvalue Markov closure: the generic
finite-matrix trace-exponential tail interface is now specialized to the
actual scaled Algorithm 1 self-adjoint dilation residual.  The one-sided
theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_elementwiseTraceResidual_ge_le`
turns the proved residual trace-MGF bound into an upper-tail eigenvalue
probability bound.  The two-sided theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_elementwiseTraceResidual_lt_ge`
uses the same trace-MGF theorem at \(\theta\) and \(-\theta\) to prove an
absolute-eigenvalue event.  These theorems do not close CACM equation (2),
because the logarithmic trace-MGF quantities are still not bounded by explicit
scalar matrix-CGF/Bernstein--Khintchine constants.

The remaining red bottleneck is now exactly the scalar matrix-CGF/log-MGF
bound for the one-sample logarithmic mean increment and its explicit
Bernstein/Khintchine constants.

Latest A1.5-B1 CFC Bernstein-parabola lift closure: the scalar-to-operator
functional-calculus bridge needed by the matrix-CGF route is now formalized.
The theorem `cstarMatrix_cfc_quadratic_eq` rewrites
\[
  \operatorname{cfc}(x\mapsto 1+\theta x+\beta x^2)(X)
\]
as \(I+\theta X+\beta X^2\) for self-adjoint finite complex C-star matrices.
The theorem `cstarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_mul_le_quadratic_of_spectrum` says that
any scalar pointwise inequality
\[
  e^{\theta x}\le 1+\theta x+\beta x^2
\]
on \(\sigma_{\mathbb R}(X)\) lifts to the Loewner inequality
\[
  \exp_{\mathrm{cfc},\mathbb R}(\theta X)
  \preceq I+\theta X+\beta X^2.
\]
Latest A1.5-B1 scalar Bernstein-parabola closure: the scalar inequality that
was left open by the CFC lift is now formalized locally.  Theorems
`real_exp_quadratic_remainder_monotone`,
`real_sq_div_two_le_exp_sub_self_sub_one_of_nonneg`, and
`real_exp_le_one_add_self_add_sq_div_two_of_nonpos` close the Taylor-remainder
facts used for the negative branch.  The theorem `real_exp_tail_two_hasSum`
identifies the degree-at-least-two exponential series tail, and
`real_exp_mul_le_quadratic_of_nonneg_of_nonneg_of_le_one` compares that tail
termwise for \(0\le x\le 1\).  The theorem
`real_exp_mul_le_quadratic_of_nonneg_of_le_one` proves
\[
  e^{a x}\le 1+a x+(e^a-a-1)x^2
\]
for \(a\ge0\) and \(x\le1\), and
`real_exp_mul_le_quadratic_scaled_of_nonneg_of_pos_of_le` proves the scaled
\(R>0\) Bernstein form.  Finally,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_mul_le_bernstein_quadratic_of_spectrum_le` combines
that scalar theorem with the CFC bridge to prove the explicit Loewner
quadratic under a real-spectrum upper bound.

Latest A1.5-B1 one-sample log-CGF closure: the generic centered
matrix-CGF/log-MGF variance-proxy theorem is now formalized locally.  The
supporting theorems
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_real_smul`,
`cstarMatrix_real_smul_isSelfAdjoint`,
`cstarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_mul_eq_normedSpace_exp_real_smul`,
`cstarMatrix_selfAdjoint_mul_self_nonneg`,
`cstarMatrix_one_add_le_normedSpace_exp_of_nonneg`, and
`cstarMatrix_log_one_add_le_self_of_nonneg` close the real-scalar expectation,
CFC/normed-exponential identification, square nonnegativity, and
\(\log(I+B)\preceq B\) side conditions.  The theorem
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_cfc_real_exp_mul_le_bernstein_variance_proxy`
proves
\[
  \mathbb E\exp_{\mathrm{cfc}}(\theta X)
  \preceq
  I+g(\theta,R)\mathbb E[X^2],
\]
and
`FiniteProbability.cstarMatrix_log_expectationCStarMatrix_normed_exp_real_smul_le_bernstein_variance_proxy`
proves the normed-exponential log-CGF form
\[
  \log\mathbb E\exp(\theta X)
  \preceq
  g(\theta,R)\mathbb E[X^2]
\]
for centered self-adjoint samples with real spectrum bounded above by \(R>0\).

Latest A1.5-B1 Algorithm 1 one-sample instantiation closure: the generic
one-sample log-CGF theorem is now instantiated with the truncated Algorithm 1
self-adjoint dilation residual increment.  The shared support-aware wrappers
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_nonneg_of_prob_pos`,
`FiniteProbability.expectationCStarMatrix_mono_of_prob_pos`, and the spectrum
bridge `cstarMatrix_spectrum_le_of_le_real_smul_one` remove the zero-mass atom
artifact.  In `ElementwiseSpectral.lean`,
`sqMagSampleProbability_expectationCStarMatrix_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_eq_zero`
proves the one-sample C-star zero-mean hypothesis,
`sqMagSampleProbability_finiteComplex_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_truncated_spectrum_le`
turns positive sampling probability into the real-spectrum upper bound
\[
  \lambda\le
  \sqrt2\left(s^{-1}\|\widehat A\|_F+
    \|\widehat A\|_F^2/(s\tau)\right),
\]
and
`sqMagSampleProbability_cstarMatrix_log_expectationCStarMatrix_normed_exp_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncated_sampleResidualIncrement_le`
proves the resulting truncated Algorithm 1 log-CGF variance-proxy theorem.

These theorems still do not close CACM equation (2): the remaining open piece
is the final Bernstein/Khintchine tail optimization and the downstream
floating-point spectral concentration transfer.

Latest A1.5-B1 parameterized Bernstein skeleton closure: the Algorithm 1
truncated trace-MGF-to-eigenvalue tail step is now formalized on both sides.
The theorem
`sqMagSampleProbability_expectationCStarMatrix_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_square_le`
proves the one-sample dilation second-moment Loewner proxy
\[
  \mathbb E[D(Z)^2]\preceq
  2mn\,\|\widehat A\|_F^2 s^{-2} I.
\]
The negative-support theorem
`sqMagSampleProbability_neg_finiteComplex_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_truncated_spectrum_le`
proves the matching spectrum upper bound for \(-D(Z)\), so the same local
one-sample Bernstein log-CGF theorem applies to the lower-tail route.  The
positive and negative scalar trace-MGF bounds
`sqMagTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncated_sampleResidualIncrement_le`
and
`sqMagTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_neg_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncated_sampleResidualIncrement_le`
give
\[
  \operatorname{tr}\exp\!\left(\sum_{t=1}^s K_t\right)
  \le (m+n)\exp\!\left(s\,g(\theta,L)V\right)
\]
in the repository's finite-real/C-star bridge notation.  The upper-tail theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_exists_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_le_exp`
and the two-sided theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncatedTraceResidual_lt_ge_exp`
combine these bounds with the existing trace-exponential Markov interfaces.

These theorems still do not close CACM equation (2): they are parameterized by
\(\theta\) and \(T\).  The remaining open piece is the parameter optimization,
the conversion to the final Drineas--Zouzias/CACM constants, and then the
downstream floating-point spectral concentration transfer.

Latest A1.5-B1 explicit failure-probability closure: the two-sided truncated
dilation eigenvalue theorem now has a concrete `1-\delta` corollary.  The
shared concentration algebra lemma
`real_exp_neg_log_two_mul_div_mul_self_add` proves that for \(B>0\) and
\(\delta>0\),
\[
  e^{-\log(2B/\delta)}B+e^{-\log(2B/\delta)}B=\delta.
\]
The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncatedTraceResidual_lt_ge_one_sub_delta`
uses this with
\[
  B=(m+n)\exp(s\,g(\theta,L)V)
\]
to prove the scaled truncated self-adjoint dilation eigenvalue event with
probability at least \(1-\delta\).  This removes the loose parameter `T` from
the high-probability statement.  The row remains open at the paper level
because the theorem is still scaled by \(\theta\), has not optimized
\(\theta\), and has not yet been converted to the rectangular spectral-norm
CACM equation (2) constants or the FP transfer theorem.

Latest A1.5-B1 scaled-radius spectral-event closure: the deterministic bridge
from the explicit scaled dilation eigenvalue event to the rectangular spectral
event is now formalized.  The shared matrix algebra lemma
`finiteLoewnerLe_of_smul_left_le_smul_id` cancels a positive scalar from a
Loewner upper bound, and the spectral theorem
`finiteLoewnerLe_smul_id_of_finiteHermitianEigenvalues_le` converts pointwise
Hermitian eigenvalue upper bounds into `M <= L I`.  The Algorithm 1 event
adapter
`algorithm1ScaledDilationAbsEigenvalueEvent_subset_exactSpectralEvent` proves
that
\[
  \forall a,\ |\lambda_a(\theta D(R))|<T
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
  \|R\|_2 \le T/\theta
\]
for \(\theta>0\) and \(T/\theta\ge0\).  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_scaled_radius`
composes this adapter with the explicit `1-\delta` tail and proves the
truncated rectangular spectral event at radius
\(\log(2B/\delta)/\theta\), with \(0<\delta\le1\) ensuring the radius is
nonnegative.  The remaining paper-level work is now the \(\theta\)
optimization/source-constant simplification and the downstream floating-point
spectral transfer.

Latest A1.5-B1 Bennett-radius closure: the theta optimization step is now
formalized as a truncated exact theorem.  The scalar theorem
`real_bernstein_exact_radius_le_of_log_le` proves that the choice
\[
  \theta=\log(1+Lr/W)/L
\]
makes the trace-exponential radius at most \(r\) whenever the standard Bennett
budget holds for \(q=\log(2(m+n)/\delta)\).  The new event monotonicity
theorems `rectOpNorm2Le_mono` and `algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_mono` compose
this scalar radius inequality with the previously proved high-probability
spectral event.  The resulting theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bennett_radius`
proves
\[
  \mathbb P\{\|\widehat A-\widetilde A\|_2\le r\}\ge1-\delta
\]
for the truncated exact Algorithm 1 sampler under the explicit Bennett budget.
The remaining paper-level work is source sample-complexity/final-constant
simplification, truncation transfer at those constants, and downstream
floating-point spectral transfer.

Latest A1.5-B1 source-sharp square variance closure: the square
Drineas--Zouzias route now avoids both the direct-dilation `sqrt 2` loss and
the loose Frobenius `2*m*n` variance proxy.  The new vector-action and
transpose-vector calculations
`sqMagProb_sum_vecNorm2Sq_rectMatMulVec_elementwiseSampleResidualIncrement_le_sharp`
and
`sqMagProb_sum_vecNorm2Sq_transposeRectMatMulVec_elementwiseSampleResidualIncrement_le_sharp`
prove the one-step second moments at row/column dimension scale.  They feed
`sqMagProb_sum_finiteQuadraticForm_rectSelfAdjointDilation_square_le_sharp_square`,
`sqMagProb_sum_rectSelfAdjointDilation_square_loewnerLe_scalar_id_sharp_square`,
and
`sqMagSampleProbability_expectationCStarMatrix_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_square_le_sharp_square`,
giving \(E[D(Z)^2]\preceq n\|A\|_F^2/s^2\,I\) for square matrices.  The
truncated source route now also has the sharp positive/negative trace-MGF
theorems
`sqMagTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncated_sampleResidualIncrement_le_sharp_square`
and
`sqMagTraceProbabilityFiniteRealTraceMGFLogBound_neg_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncated_sampleResidualIncrement_le_sharp_square`,
plus the two-sided tail skeletons
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncatedTraceResidual_lt_ge_exp_sharp_square`
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteHermitianEigenvalue_scaled_rectSelfAdjointDilation_truncatedTraceResidual_lt_ge_one_sub_delta_sharp_square`.
The full CACM equation (2) row remains open because the source
sample-complexity/final-constant simplification, final truncation transfer,
and floating-point spectral transfer are still not composed.

Latest A1.5-B1 source-sharp Bennett closure: the source-sharp square
two-sided eigenvalue skeleton now has the same deterministic spectral and
scalar Bennett conversions as the older loose-constant route.  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_scaled_radius_sharp_square`
converts the sharp scaled dilation eigenvalue event into the rectangular
spectral event at radius \(\log(2B/\delta)/\theta\).  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bennett_radius_sharp_square`
then chooses \(\theta=\log(1+Lr/W)/L\) and proves a radius-\(r\) event under
the explicit source-sharp Bennett budget with
\(L=s^{-1}\|\widehat A\|_F+\|\widehat A\|_F^2/(s\tau)\) and
\(W=s\,n\|\widehat A\|_F^2/s^2\).  The remaining paper-level work is now the
algebraic simplification of this budget to the Drineas--Zouzias/CACM
sample-complexity constants, followed by truncation and floating-point
transfer at those constants.

Latest A1.5-B1 conservative denominator closure: the scalar bridge
`real_bennett_transform_lower_bound_two_add` proves the fallback inequality
\(x^2/(2+x)\le(1+x)\log(1+x)-x\), and
`real_bennett_budget_of_quadratic_denominator_two_add` turns
\(q\le r^2/(2W+Lr)\) into the source-sharp Bennett budget.  The resulting
Algorithm 1 theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_bernstein_denominator_sharp_square`
is a fully proved high-probability spectral-event corollary under the
conservative denominator.  It is not advertised as the CACM equation (2)
constant closure: the sharper \(2W+\frac23Lr\) denominator and the final
source sample-size algebra remain open.

Latest A1.5-B1 source sample-budget closure: the sharper scalar denominator
route is now formalized by `real_bennett_transform_lower_bound_two_add_two_thirds`
and `real_bennett_budget_of_quadratic_denominator_two_add_two_thirds`.  The
Algorithm 1 theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_truncatedTraceResidual_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_sharp_square`
proves the truncated exact spectral event from
\(14n\|A\|_F^2\log(2(2n)/\delta)\le s\varepsilon^2\).  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactTruncatedSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_sharp_square`
then composes the deterministic truncation transfer and gives the exact event
against the original matrix at radius \(\varepsilon\).  The theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_sharp_square`
transfers the same probability to the rounded truncated sketch under an
explicit entrywise perturbation budget \(B\).

Latest A1.5-B1 floating-point gamma-budget closure: the explicit perturbation
budget is now derived locally on the sampler's probability-one support, and the
advertised scalar-radius corollaries expand the internal budget matrix to
\(\varepsilon+n(\|\widehat A\|_F^2/\tau)\gamma_{s+1}\) and then to
\(\varepsilon+(2n^2\|A\|_F^2/\varepsilon)\gamma_{s+1}\).  The
sampling lemmas `hitCount_le_steps`, `hitCount_eq_zero_of_forall_not_hit`,
`fl_elementwiseTraceSketch_zero_init_eq_zero_of_forall_not_hit`, and
`sqMagTraceErrorBudget_nonneg` close the small deterministic support
dependencies.  The support-aware theorem
`fl_elementwiseTraceSketch_zero_init_sqMag_error_bound_of_positiveProb` proves
the entrywise budget with `Q=s` on traces that sample only positive-probability
entries.  The final theorem
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralEvent_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_gamma_square`
intersects the exact source-budget event with that probability-one support
event and proves the rounded truncated spectral event with no global `hPoint`
hypothesis.  The scalar-radius theorems
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_gamma_square`
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1FlTruncatedSpectralRadius_ge_one_sub_delta_source_sample_budget_explicit_gamma_square`
make the final FP term readable in dimensions and unit roundoff.  The cited
square source-aligned Algorithm 1 theorem is now closed for exact and FP
arithmetic under `gammaValid fp s` and `gammaValid fp (s+1)`.

Latest A1.5-KH1 finite-test Khintchine foundation: the generic exact
Rademacher signed-matrix quadratic-form primitive is now formalized in
`Preconditioning.lean`.  The theorem
`finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_matrix_sum_eq_sum` proves the
deterministic identity
\[
  z^\mathsf T\Bigl(\sum_k \omega_k M_k\Bigr)z
  =\sum_k (z^\mathsf T M_k z)\omega_k
\]
for a finite real matrix family, and
`rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_signed_matrix_sum_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div`
applies the local simultaneous two-sided Rademacher Hoeffding theorem to a
finite test family.  This is exact-probability and exact-arithmetic
infrastructure only; it does not charge any floating-point computation and does
not yet prove the source-uniform Algorithm 1 spectral-norm tail.

Latest A1.5-KH2 Algorithm 1 Rademacherized copy-difference adapter: the
finite-test theorem is now generalized to arbitrary finite matrix index types
by `finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_matrix_sum_eq_sum_fintype` and
`rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_signed_matrix_sum_fintype_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div`.
`ElementwiseSpectral.lean` instantiates that general form as
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_forall_abs_finiteQuadraticForm_rademacher_signed_rectSelfAdjointDilation_sampleResidualIncrement_diff_le_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div`,
where the finite matrix index is the Algorithm 1 dilation index
`Fin m ⊕ Fin n` and the signed summands are the fixed stepwise
independent-copy residual-increment differences.  This closes the exact
Rademacher finite-test layer attached to the actual Algorithm 1 increments;
the remaining step is still the spectral-tail upgrade from finite tests to the
all-copy-differences operator event.

Latest A1.5-KH3 finite-cover Rademacher operator adapter: `MatrixAlgebra.lean`
now defines `finiteUnitBallCover` and proves
`finiteLoewnerLe_of_finite_unit_ball_cover_quadraticForm`, a deterministic
exact bridge from finite quadratic-form tests to a scalar-identity Loewner
upper bound with explicit radius \(\eta+L(2\rho+\rho^2)\).  `ElementwiseSpectral.lean`
then proves
`rademacherTraceProbability_eventProb_rectOpNorm2Le_signed_sampleResidualIncrement_diff_ge_one_sub_sum_two_mul_exp_neg_sq_div_of_finiteUnitBallCover`,
which combines that cover bridge with the A1.5-KH2 finite-test Rademacher tail
to obtain an exact rectangular operator event for fixed signed copy-difference
sums.  This is still intermediate exact-law infrastructure: the coarse
signed-dilation operator radius \(L\) is visible and deterministic, not a
probability tail or computed certificate, and the source-uniform all-copy-
differences event remains open.

Latest A1.5-KH4 literal all-copy-differences obstruction:
`ElementwiseSpectral.lean` proves
`algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_trace_residual_small_unit_diff_not_rectOpNorm2Le`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_ge`,
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_pos`;
the quantitative wrapper
`sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactAllCopyDiffSpectralEvent_smallEntry_delta_ge`
also shows that any claimed \(1-\delta\) lower bound for this all-copy event
must satisfy \(p_{\rm tiny}\le\delta\).
For the one-step literal input \([1,(|L|+2)^{-1}]\), the residual difference
between the tiny-entry trace and the unit-entry trace violates the proposed
operator radius \(L\), and the exact squared-magnitude law assigns the
all-copy-differences failure event probability at least the tiny-entry
sampling probability.  This is exact-law obstruction evidence: the literal
untruncated all-copy support event cannot be used as a probability-one
prerequisite, and a final CACM equation (2) route must supply a genuine tail
or keep reciprocal-entry, truncation, or no-small-entry dependence visible.

Latest LS.2g-en equation (8) active/prefix global-product assembly: the theorems

Latest LS.2g-eu zero-relative-budget compact-product endpoint: the compact
product side condition now has an exact/zero-compact discharge theorem,
`storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget_lt_one_of_relativeBudget_eq_zero`.  If
the stored compact sequence relative budget is exactly zero, every finite

compact-product budget is strictly below one.  This closes a listed dependency

bottleneck.  It does not prove the positive floating-point compact-product
smallness inequality when the relative budget is positive, and it does not

conditioning data, norm-square nonbreakdown, or the final generic equation (8)
QR/preconditioner theorem.  Two weak-component passes are clean: focused

Latest LS.2g-ev positive relative-budget cap for canonical compact-product
smallness: the least-squares QR layer now proves
`storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget_lt_one_of_diagDominant_finite_max_relativeBudget_le`.
If local diagonal dominance holds, the stored compact sequence relative budget is
bounded by a nonnegative scalar `cmax`, and the canonical scalar product
inequality is true with `cmax` in place of the exact relative budget, then the
finite global compact-product budget is strictly below one.  This is a listed
positive-budget compact-product dependency reduction: it replaces the exact
relative budget by an explicit source-facing cap.  It does not prove the cap,
the scalar inequality, local diagonal dominance/off-diagonal control,
determinant or conditioning data, norm-square nonbreakdown, or the final generic
equation (8) QR/preconditioner theorem.  First weak-component validation is

LSQRSolve build, executable lookup, `git diff --check`, touched Lean marker
scan, qualified axiom audit, theorem PDF compile, targeted `pdftotext`, and
post-compile rendered-page inspection all passed again.  This checkpoint now
has two consecutive clean passes.

Latest LS.2g-ew uniform per-step compact-panel cap reduction: the QR layer now
proves `storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget_le_mul_of_step_le`, and the
least-squares QR layer now proves
`storedQRCompactSequenceProductBudget_lt_one_of_diagDominant_finite_max_stepBudget_le`.
Together these show that a uniform one-step compact-panel relative cap
`cStep` gives the sequence cap `n * cStep`, and that the canonical
finite-max compact-product condition may be checked with that explicit cap.
This is a listed positive-budget compact-product dependency reduction only:
it does not prove the one-step cap, the scalar inequality using `n * cStep`,
local diagonal dominance/off-diagonal control, determinant/conditioning data,

`LeastSquaresSketch.lean` now proves
`leverageTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_rowSampleLSObjective_le_one_add_eta_of_augmentedSpan_sample_budget_of_objective_error_and_stored_qr_rowMaxDiagDefect_globalProduct_solver_of_actualUnitRoundoff_no_gammaValid`.
These wrappers derive the local and sampled `gammaValid` guards from
`(m : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` or `(s : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`, keeping the scalar row-defect,
determinant, norm-square nonbreakdown, and global compact-product fields
visible.  Two weak-component passes are clean in the not-proved ledger.  This is
a validity-surface reduction only; it does not prove the row-defect condition,
determinant/nonbreakdown fields, global compact-product smallness, conditioning
fields, or the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-gf finite stage-diagonal lower-bound defect packaging:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now defines `storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget`, proves
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefect_le_budget`, and proves
`storedQRStageBudget_le_diag_of_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos`.  It also
adds the source-control and local solver wrappers

`StoredQRSourceOffDiagonalControl.of_stored_trailing_sequence_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_signedStageUniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_completedColumns_globalProduct_stageDiagDefect_offdiag_rows`, and
`LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_signedStageUniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_completedColumns_globalProduct_stageDiagDefect_offdiag_rows`.
These theorems replace the active/prefix diagonal lower-bound family
`stageBudget k <= |(S_k)_{ii}|` for displayed rows `i < k` by the scalar finite
condition `storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget <= 0`.  This is a listed
red-bottleneck dependency reduction only: the scalar stage-diagonal condition,
determinant and nonbreakdown fields, global compact-product smallness, and the
final generic QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-gg active-max-pivot stage-diagonal solver reduction:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves `storedQRActiveMaxPivotColumn_pivotMax`,
`StoredQRSourceOffDiagonalControl.of_stored_trailing_sequence_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_normSqBudget_signedStageUniformBudget_product_of_normSqBudget_activePrefix_activeBlockRecurrence_globalCompactBudget_activeMaxPivot_completedColumns_globalProduct_stageDiagDefect_offdiag_rows`,

`LSQRSolveBackwardError.of_stored_trailing_householder_sequence_topBlock_fl_backSub_gamma_bound_explicitCompactBudget_of_signed_alpha_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_signedStageUniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_activeMaxPivot_completedColumns_globalProduct_stageDiagDefect_offdiag_rows`.
These theorems combine the LS.2g-gf scalar stage-diagonal defect surface with
the finite active-pivot policy: the raw pivot-maximality field is derived from
the equation that the displayed pivot is `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn`.
This is a listed pivot-policy reduction only; the scalar stage-diagonal
condition, determinant/nonbreakdown and conditioning fields, global
compact-product smallness, and the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem
remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-gh exact no-pivot stage-diagonal scalar route elimination:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`exactHouseholderQRDiagDominanceCounterexample_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_pos`
and
`not_forall_exact_trailing_householder_sequence_implies_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos`.
For the existing two-stage exact no-pivot Householder counterexample, the
constant stage budget `2` gives a positive scalar stage-diagonal lower-bound
defect at displayed stage one.  Consequently exact trailing recurrence, valid
signed squared-norm identities, and nonzero Householder denominators cannot
prove `storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget <= 0`, even with this fixed natural
budget.  This is a route-elimination result only; the scalar stage-diagonal
condition, determinant/nonbreakdown and conditioning fields, global
compact-product smallness, and the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem
remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-hl exact no-pivot comparison scalar route elimination:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`exactHouseholderQRDiagDominanceCounterexample_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_pos`
and
`not_forall_exact_trailing_householder_sequence_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`.
For the existing two-stage exact no-pivot Householder counterexample, the
constant stage budget `3` gives a positive scalar stage-budget/row-max
comparison defect at displayed stage one.  Consequently exact trailing
recurrence, valid signed squared-norm identities, nonzero Householder
denominators, and nonnegative stage budgets cannot prove
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget <= 0`.  This is a route-elimination
result only; the comparison scalar, local diagonal dominance/off-diagonal
control, active-pivot policy, signed-stage recurrence budgets, finite-max
smallness, and the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or
visible.

Latest LS.2g-hm active scalar-comparison source-denominator compact-product handoff:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves the source-control wrapper
`StoredQRSourceOffDiagonalControl.of_stored_trailing_sequence_diagDominant_signedStageUniformBudget_globalCompactBudget_activeMaxPivot_completedColumns_finiteMaxSourceDenURationalGammaCanonicalBounds_stageRowMaxComparisonDefect_offdiag_rows`
and the local solver wrapper

These theorems use the existing canonical source-denominator rational-gamma
finite-max product theorem to derive the raw compact-product field internally
from source-denominator nonbreakdown, a unit-roundoff cap, `(m : ℝ) * Ucap < 1`,
and the displayed canonical rational-gamma cap-smallness inequality.  Thus the
active diagonal-dominant scalar-comparison local source-control/solver branch
no longer exposes the assembled finite-max scalar involving
`storedQRCompactSequenceRelativeBudget`.  This is a compact-product
theorem-surface reduction only; source-denominator nonbreakdown, unit-roundoff
cap and scalar cap-smallness obligations, local diagonal dominance, the scalar
comparison defect, active-pivot policy, signed-stage recurrence budgets, and
the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-hn sampled active scalar-comparison source-denominator handoff:

wrapper

obligations, and the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or
visible.

Latest LS.2g-hq stored-recurrence actual-unit scalar-smallness route
elimination:
`not_forall_diagDominant_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_no_gammaValid_implies_uRationalGammaCanonicalSmallness`
shows that the post-LS.2g-hp stored-lower surface still cannot derive the
canonical rational-gamma scalar smallness inequality.  The witness is the
`1 x 1` exact-with-unit-roundoff model with the first stored panel update
made explicit in `A_hat 1`; it satisfies the stored recurrence, local diagonal
dominance, signed-alpha equation, and `(1 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`, while the finite
scalar expression is still too large.  This classifies the actual-unit scalar
smallness field as a genuine numerical/domain obligation unless a stronger
scale, conditioning, or concrete machine-model theorem is supplied.

Latest LS.2g-hr stored-recurrence actual-unit diagonal-dominance route
elimination:
`not_forall_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_implies_diagDominant`
shows that the stored panel recurrence, signed-alpha equation, nonzero source
denominators, and `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` still cannot derive local diagonal
dominance.  The witness is the existing `2 x 2` Householder diagonal-dominance
counterexample, now lifted through
`exactHouseholderQRDiagDominanceCounterexample_stored_step` using the exact
FP model `exactHouseholderQRDiagDominanceCounterexampleFP` with `fp.u = 0`.
This classifies local diagonal dominance/off-diagonal control as a genuine
loop/domain invariant, not a hidden consequence of the stored-lower
actual-unit recurrence.

Latest LS.2g-hs stored-recurrence actual-unit active-pivot route
elimination:
`not_forall_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_implies_activeMaxPivotChoice`
shows that the stored panel recurrence, signed-alpha equation, nonzero source
denominators, and `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` also cannot derive the displayed
active-pivot policy.  The same `2 x 2` witness starts from
`[[1,2],[0,1]]`, where the active trailing norm of the second column is
larger than that of the displayed first pivot.  The concrete lemma
`exactHouseholderQRDiagDominanceCounterexample_not_activeMaxPivotChoice`
turns `householderActiveMaxPivotColumn_pivot_max` into the contradiction
`5 <= 1`.  This classifies the active-pivot equation as a genuine
pivoted/sorted-loop invariant or visible assumption, not a hidden consequence
of the unpivoted stored-lower actual-unit recurrence.

Latest LS.2g-ht stored-recurrence actual-unit scalar row-budget route
elimination:
`not_forall_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_implies_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos`
and
`not_forall_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the stored panel recurrence, signed-alpha equation, nonzero source
denominators, and `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` cannot derive the scalar
stage-diagonal lower-bound condition or the scalar stage-budget/row-max
comparison condition.  The same exact FP witness with `fp.u = 0` satisfies the
stored recurrence and source-denominator facts; constant stage budgets `2` and
`3` make the scalar stage-diagonal and comparison defects positive.  This
classifies both scalar fields as genuine pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-control
loop invariants or visible assumptions, not hidden consequences of the
unpivoted stored-lower actual-unit recurrence.

Latest LS.2g-hu diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence actual-unit scalar
row-budget route elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_stored_step`,
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_diagDominant`,
`not_forall_diagDominant_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos`,
and
`not_forall_diagDominant_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that even local diagonal dominance does not make the scalar row-budget
fields automatic on the stored-panel actual-unit surface.  The exact stored
sequence from `[[3,2],[0,1]]` keeps the displayed leading blocks diagonally
dominant, satisfies the stored recurrence, signed-alpha equation, source
denominators, `(2 : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`, and nonnegative stage budgets, but stage
budgets `4` and `3` make the scalar diagonal-lower and comparison defects
positive.  This rules out the diagonal-dominant shortcut; the scalar fields
must still come from a concrete loop invariant or remain visible.

Latest LS.2g-if active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence comparison
route elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_activeMaxPivotChoice` and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same exact stored sequence from `[[3,2],[0,1]]` also satisfies
the finite active max-pivot selector policy.  Therefore the route "stored
recurrence plus signed-alpha/source-denominator/actual-unit facts plus local
diagonal dominance plus active max-pivoting plus nonnegative stage budgets"
still cannot derive the scalar stage-budget/row-max comparison condition.  The
comparison scalar remains a genuine loop invariant for a stronger
pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-controlled proof, or a visible assumption.

Latest LS.2g-ig compact-product active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence
comparison route elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_rhs_step`,
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_compactSequenceRelativeBudget_eq_zero`,
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_compactSequenceProductBudget_lt_one`,
and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_product_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same witness also satisfies a zero RHS stored recurrence and the
global compact-product smallness condition in exact arithmetic.  Therefore the
route "stored recurrence plus stored RHS recurrence plus
signed-alpha/source-denominator/actual-unit facts plus local diagonal dominance
plus active max-pivoting plus compact-product smallness plus nonnegative stage
budgets" still cannot derive the scalar stage-budget/row-max comparison
condition.  Compact-product smallness is not the missing comparison invariant.

Latest LS.2g-ih finite-max active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence
comparison route elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_finiteMaxSmallness` and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_finiteMaxSmallness_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same exact/zero-RHS witness also satisfies the canonical
finite-max compact-product scalar used to derive the active branch's global
product field.  The compact relative budget is zero, so the finite-max scalar
reduces to `0 < 1`; nevertheless the scalar stage-budget/row-max comparison
defect remains positive.  The finite-max scalar is not the missing comparison
invariant either.

Latest LS.2g-ii signed-stage global-budget finite-max active-pivot
diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence comparison route elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_globalCompactBudget_recurrence`
and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_globalCompactBudget_finiteMaxSmallness_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same exact/zero-RHS witness can also satisfy the finite signed
global compact-budget recurrence.  The witness uses the nonconstant
nonnegative stage budget `0, 3, 10, ...`; the global compact-update budgets
are zero in exact arithmetic, and `coxHighamActiveRowGrowthFactor 2 <= 3`
handles the only nontrivial recurrence step.  The scalar comparison defect is
still positive at stage one, so signed-stage global recurrence budgets are not
the missing comparison invariant either.

Latest LS.2g-ij row-max-granted signed-stage global-budget finite-max
active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence comparison route elimination:

and

show that the same exact/zero-RHS witness also satisfies the finite
row-max/diagonal defect scalar.  Local diagonal dominance packages that scalar
through `storedQRRowMaxDiagDefectBudget_nonpos_of_diagDominant`, while the
nonconstant stage budget `0, 3, 10, ...` keeps the scalar comparison defect

Latest LS.2g-ik determinant- and row-max-granted signed-stage global-budget
finite-max active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence comparison route
elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero`
and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_globalCompactBudget_finiteMaxSmallness_rowMaxDiagDefect_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same exact/zero-RHS witness also satisfies nonsingular displayed

invariant either.

Latest LS.2g-il conditioning- and row-max-granted signed-stage global-budget
finite-max active-pivot diagonal-dominant stored-recurrence comparison route
elimination:
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexample_compactComponentBudget_eq_zero`,
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexampleKappaBudget`,
`storedDiagDominantComparisonCounterexampleKappaNormSqBudget`, and
`not_forall_diagDominant_activeMaxPivot_leadingBlock_det_ne_zero_kappaInf_selfNorm_dualBudget_globalCompactBudget_finiteMaxSmallness_rowMaxDiagDefect_signedAlphaDef_stored_trailing_sequence_actualU_sourceDen_nonnegStageBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
show that the same exact/zero-RHS witness also satisfies the local conditioning
and dual compact-budget fields used by the `kappaInf` route.  The compact
component budget is zero in the exact model, and the local positive `K` budget
is the exact squared self-norm expression plus one; all determinant- and
row-max-granted signed-stage fields hold, but the scalar comparison defect
remains positive at stage one.  Thus conditioning and dual compact-budget data
are not the missing comparison invariant either.

Latest LS.2g-hv combined row-sorting/column-pivot bookkeeping:
`rectLSGram_permuteRowsCols`, `rectLSRhs_permuteRowsCols`,
`RectLSNormalEquations.of_permuteRowsCols`, `lsResidual_permuteRowsCols`,
`lsObjective_permuteRowsCols`, and
`IsLeastSquaresMinimizer.of_permuteRowsCols` package the row-permutation and
column-pivot relabeling layers together.  They show that a row-sorted and
column-pivoted least-squares normal-equation solution or exact minimizer maps
back to the original coordinates by undoing only the column permutation, while
the row permutation preserves the residual objective and normal-equation data.
This closes Cox--Higham bookkeeping for the pivoted/sorted handoff only; it
does not prove the exact pivoted/sorted Householder growth/sorting field,
diagonal lower bounds, compact smallness, or the final QR/preconditioner
theorem.  Full validation is clean: focused builds, executable lookup, axiom
audit, two PDF compiles to 270 pages, targeted `pdftotext`, rendered pages 138
and 268, `git diff --check`, touched Lean marker scan, broad `lake build`, and
temporary-file cleanup all passed.

Latest LS.2g-hw signed-stage global-budget monotonicity bridge:
`storedQRSignedStageGlobalCompactBudget_nonneg` proves the finite maximum
compact-update budget is nonnegative under `gammaValid fp m`, and
`storedQRSignedStageBudget_mono_on_stages_of_globalCompactBudget` proves that
the scalar recurrence
`coxHighamActiveRowGrowthFactor m * B_t + Delta_t^max <= B_{t+1}`, together
with nonnegative stage budgets, makes the budget sequence monotone on the
algorithmic range `a <= b <= n`.  This is a scoped recurrence-surface
reduction only; it does not prove the diagonal-lower/comparison invariant,
compact-product smallness, active-pivot policy, or the final
QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-hx horizon-clamped global-product budget surface:
`qrStageHorizonBudget` freezes a stage-budget sequence after the QR horizon,
and `qrStageHorizonBudget_mono_of_mono_on_stages` turns monotonicity on
`b <= n` into ordinary global monotonicity for that clamped sequence.  The
completed-column global-product source-control and solver wrappers with suffix
`_of_horizonBudget` use this clamped budget to remove the separate global
monotonicity field from that branch.  Validation is clean: focused
`lake build LeanFpAnalysis.FP.Algorithms.LeastSquares.LSQRSolve`, executable
lookup, qualified axiom audit reporting only standard `propext`,
`Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`, two theorem-PDF compiles to 271 pages,
whole-PDF `pdftotext`, rendered inspection of pages 206 and 265,
`git diff --check`, touched Lean marker scan, broad `lake build`, and
temporary-file cleanup all passed.  This is still a theorem-surface
reduction only; diagonal lower bounds, determinant/norm-square nonbreakdown,
active-pivot policy, compact-product smallness, and the final
QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-gi row-max-to-stage-diagonal scalar bridge:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos_of_rowMaxDiagDefectBudget_nonpos_stageBudget_le_rowMax`
and
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos_of_diagDominant_stageBudget_le_rowMax`.
The first theorem turns nonpositive row-max scalar defect into nonpositive
stage-diagonal scalar defect whenever the uniform stage budget is bounded by
the displayed strict-upper row maximum on each row `i < k`; the second theorem
specializes this bridge to local diagonal dominance via the existing row-max
defect theorem.  This is a reusable dependency bridge only: the concrete
pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-controlled loop still has to prove the
stage-budget/row-max comparison, determinant/nonbreakdown and conditioning
fields, global compact-product smallness, or keep those assumptions visible in
the final QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-gj active-pivot stage-budget/row-max comparison route elimination:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_stageBudget_le_rowMax`.
The theorem reuses the finite active-pivot row-budget counterexample sequence
and chooses the oversized stage budget `3`.  Upper triangular nonsingular
displayed blocks, positive active-block mass, active max-pivoting, active-block
budget control, and displayed strict-upper budget control all hold, but at
displayed stage one the actual strict-upper row maximum is `2`, so the
comparison `stageBudget <= rowMax` fails.  This rules out a listed shortcut
left by the LS.2g-gi bridge: the stage-budget/row-max comparison is not a
consequence of the current active-pivot/budget surface and must be proved by a
stronger pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-control invariant or kept visible as a
domain assumption.  The scalar stage-diagonal condition, determinant/nonbreakdown
and conditioning fields, global compact-product smallness, and final generic
QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or visible.

Latest LS.2g-hb row-max-alone stage-diagonal route elimination:

The witness from LS.2g-ha remains diagonally safe for the row-max scalar defect,

hypothesis by the scalar actual-unit condition `(m : ℝ) * fp.u < 1`, deriving
the former validity field internally with `gammaValid_of_u_le_cap`.  It is a
source-level validity-surface reduction only: row-max defect, the
stage-budget/row-max comparison, determinant/nonbreakdown or conditioning
fields, dual compact-budget data, compact-product smallness, and the final
QR/preconditioner theorem remain open or visibly scoped.

Latest LS.2g-gw finite stage-diagonal converse packaging:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos_of_stageBudget_le_diag`.
This theorem is the converse finite-maximum package for the active/prefix
stage-diagonal route: if the displayed pointwise diagonal lower-bound family
`stageBudget k <= |(S_k)_{ii}|` holds for every off-diagonal row `i < k`, then
the scalar finite defect
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget hmn A_hat stageBudget` is nonpositive.
Together with `storedQRStageBudget_le_diag_of_stageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos`,
the scalar condition is now formalized in both directions for the finite
displayed family.  This closes a dependency packaging direction only: the
concrete pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal-controlled loop still has to prove that
pointwise family, derive it from row-max data, or keep the scalar/pointwise
condition visible.

Latest LS.2g-gx finite stage-budget/row-max comparison packaging:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now defines
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget` and proves
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefect_le_budget`,
`storedQRStageBudget_le_rowMax_of_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`,
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos_of_stageBudget_le_rowMax`,
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos_of_rowMaxDiagDefectBudget_nonpos_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`,
and
`storedQRStageDiagLowerDefectBudget_nonpos_of_diagDominant_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`.
The scalar budget is the finite maximum of `stageBudget k - rowMax(k,i)` over
displayed off-diagonal rows `i < k`; nonpositivity extracts the comparison
family, and the converse packages any pointwise comparison proof back into the
scalar condition.  This closes the comparison-packaging direction of the
row-max stage-diagonal bridge only: the concrete pivoted/sorted/off-diagonal
loop still has to prove the comparison defect, prove the row-max defect, or
keep those conditions visible.

and
`leverageTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_rowSampleLSObjective_le_one_add_eta_of_augmentedSpan_sample_budget_of_objective_error_and_stored_qr_activePrefix_globalProduct_activeMaxPivot_rowMaxDiagDefect_stageRowMaxComparisonDefect_kappaInf_dualBudget_solver_of_actualUnitRoundoff_no_gammaValid`.
These theorems replace the displayed `stageBudget <= rowMax` comparison family
by the scalar finite condition

with

`leverageTraceProbability_eventProb_fl_rowSampleLSObjective_le_one_add_eta_of_augmentedSpan_sample_budget_of_objective_error_and_stored_qr_activePrefix_globalProduct_activeMaxPivot_rowMaxDiagDefect_stageRowMaxComparisonDefect_kappaInf_dualBudget_solver_of_actualUnitRoundoff_no_gammaValid_of_horizonBudget`
now derives sampled validity from `(s : ℝ) * fp.u < 1` and calls the new

That explicit-validity sibling extracts the same scalar comparison and calls

keeping `gammaValid` fields visible; the actual-unit sibling removes both
sampled validity and monotonicity.  Validation is clean: focused

executable lookup, standard-only axiom audit, theorem-PDF compiles, whole-PDF
text extraction, rendered scalar-comparison and not-proved pages,
`git diff --check`, touched Lean marker scan, broad `lake build`, and
temporary-file cleanup all passed.
This closes the scalar-comparison theorem-surface dependency only:
the concrete loop still has to prove the row-max defect, prove the comparison
defect, prove determinant/nonbreakdown or conditioning and compact-product
smallness, or keep those fields visible in the final QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-gz scalar-comparison route elimination:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
and
`not_forall_leadingBlock_upper_det_product_activeBlockPos_activeMaxPivot_activeBlockBudget_offdiagBudget_implies_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`.
These wrappers lift the already-checked displayed-comparison obstructions to
the finite scalar condition itself: if the active-pivot budget surface, or the
same surface plus product compact-smallness, implied
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget <= 0`, then
`storedQRStageBudget_le_rowMax_of_stageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget_nonpos`
would supply the pointwise comparison theorem already refuted by the
active-pivot oversized-budget witness.  This is a route-elimination result
only: it does not prove the scalar comparison defect, row-max defect,
determinant/nonbreakdown or conditioning fields, global compact-product
smallness, or the final generic QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-ha row-max-granted scalar-comparison route elimination:
`LSQRSolve.lean` now proves
`activeMaxPivotRowMaxComparisonCounterexample_rowMaxDiagDefectBudget_nonpos`,

The new witness has nonpositive scalar row-max defect but positive scalar

active/off-diagonal budget surface prove
`storedQRStageRowMaxComparisonDefectBudget <= 0`.  Product compact-smallness
still does not rescue the route.  This is a route-elimination result only: the
comparison defect must be proved by a stronger concrete loop invariant or kept
visible in the final QR/preconditioner theorem.

Latest LS.2g-hk active-surface scalar-comparison route elimination:

Latest A1.5-KH5 sample-count exact-spectral obstruction:
`FiniteProbability.lean` now proves `FiniteProbability.prob_le_eventProb_of_mem`.
`ElementwiseSpectral.lean` now proves
`sqMagTraceProbMass_algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_all_tiny`,
`algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_all_tiny_trace_residual_not_rectOpNorm2Le`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_ge`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_delta_ge`,
`sqMagProb_algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_small_lt_one`,
`log_inv_delta_le_nat_mul_log_inv_of_pow_le`,
`log_inv_delta_div_log_inv_le_nat_of_pow_le`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_log_delta_le`,
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_sample_count_ge`.
The contradiction wrappers
`sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_of_delta_lt_pow`
and
`sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_of_sample_count_lt`
record the contrapositive forms directly.
The probability-surface wrappers
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_le_one_sub_pow`,
`sqMagTraceProbability_eventProb_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry_lt_one`,
and
`exists_delta_not_sqMagTraceProbability_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_all_tiny_smallEntry`
also record the direct success cap and fixed-sample impossibility.
The concrete source-budget witness theorems
`real_log_180000_le_18`,
`algorithm1RectSourceBudget_1_2_100_one_div_30000`,
`algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_100_small_prob_gt_one_div_30000`,
`algorithm1SmallEntrySupportMatrix_100_rect_source_budget_one_div_30000`, and
`sqMagTraceProbability_not_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_rect_source_budget_witness`
show that, for \(m=1\), \(n=2\), \(s=1\), \(L=100\),
\(\delta=1/30000\), and \(A=[1,1/102]\), the rectangular source-style
sample budget is true while the literal exact spectral-event lower bound is
formally impossible.
The wrapper
`not_forall_algorithm1ExactSpectralEvent_of_rect_source_budget_one_div_30000`
refutes the universal source-budget-only implication at those fixed
parameters for all exact inputs with positive squared-magnitude denominator.
For the literal input `[1,(|L|+2)^{-1}]` and any positive sample count `s`,
the exact all-tiny trace has product-law mass `p_tiny ^ s`, lies outside the
exact radius-`L` spectral event, and forces any claimed `1 - delta` lower
bound for that exact spectral event to satisfy `p_tiny ^ s <= delta`.  For
positive `delta`, the same obstruction yields the logarithmic necessary
conditions `log(1/delta) <= s * log(1/p_tiny)` and
`log(1/delta)/log(1/p_tiny) <= s`, hence a fixed-`L`
one-sided order requirement `s = Omega_L(log(1/delta))`.  Equivalently,
`P(E_spec) <= 1 - p_tiny ^ s < 1`; if either
`delta < p_tiny ^ s` or the divided sample-count lower bound is violated, the
claimed event lower bound is formally impossible.  This is
exact-law/exact-arithmetic obstruction evidence only; it introduces no
floating-point computation and does not close the source-uniform CACM equation
(2) matrix-tail theorem.
